 3404 # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 3405 + # ROLE/TITLE-BASED PROSPECTING (#7) — Serper + Claude

 3406 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 3407 +

 3408 + def scan_role_targets ( states= None , progress_callback= None ) -> list :

 3409 + """

 3410 + Scan for K- 12 contacts with specific CS/CTE/STEM leadership titles.

 3411 + Only searches rare/specific titles — common titles (Principal, Teacher)

 3412 + are too noisy for web search and are handled by job posting scanner.

 3413 + Uses Serper + Claude Haiku. Returns list of signal dicts.

 3414 + Cost: ~$ 2.50 /scan ( 48 Serper queries + 1 Claude call).

 3415 + """

 3416 + if not SERPER_API_KEY:

 3417 + logger.warning( "SERPER_API_KEY not set — cannot scan role targets" )

 3418 + return []

 3419 +

 3420 + import httpx

 3421 +

 3422 + _load_nces_lookup()

 3423 + _load_cross_references()

 3424 +

 3425 + scan_states = states or list (TERRITORY_STATES)

 3426 + all_snippets = []

 3427 + seen_urls = set ()

 3428 +

 3429 + if progress_callback:

 3430 + progress_callback( f"Searching { len (scan_states)} states for CS/CTE/STEM leaders (~$2.50)..." )

 3431 +

 3432 + for state_abbr in scan_states:

 3433 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_abbr, state_abbr)

 3434 + queries = [

 3435 + f'"Director of Computer Science" OR "CS Director" OR "CTE Director" OR "Director of CTE" OR "STEM Director" OR "Director of STEM" school district " {state_name} "' ,

 3436 + f'"Director of Technology" OR "EdTech Director" OR "Instructional Technology Director" OR "Educational Technology Director" school district " {state_name} "' ,

 3437 + f'"Curriculum Director" OR "Director of Curriculum" OR "STEM Coordinator" OR "CTE Coordinator" school district " {state_name} "' ,

 3438 + f'"Cybersecurity Teacher" OR "Game Design Teacher" OR "Esports Coach" OR "Robotics Teacher" school district " {state_name} "' ,

 3439 + ]

 3440 + for query in queries:

 3441 + try :

 3442 + resp = httpx.post(

 3443 + SERPER_URL,

 3444 + headers={ "X-API-Key" : SERPER_API_KEY, "Content-Type" : "application/json" },

 3445 + json={ "q" : query, "num" : 10 , "tbs" : "qdr:y" }, # Last year — roles persist

 3446 + timeout= 15.0 ,

 3447 + )

 3448 + data = resp.json()

 3449 + for item in data.get( "organic" , [])[: 10 ]:

 3450 + url = item.get( "link" , "" )

 3451 + if url and url not in seen_urls:

 3452 + seen_urls.add(url)

 3453 + all_snippets.append({

 3454 + "title" : item.get( "title" , "" ),

 3455 + "snippet" : item.get( "snippet" , "" ),

 3456 + "url" : url,

 3457 + "searched_state" : state_abbr,

 3458 + })

 3459 + time.sleep( 0.5 )

 3460 + except Exception as e:

 3461 + logger.warning( f"Serper role scan failed for {state_abbr} : {e} " )

 3462 +

 3463 + if not all_snippets:

 3464 + logger.info( "Role scan: no results found" )

 3465 + if progress_callback:

 3466 + progress_callback( "Role scan: no results found." )

 3467 + return []

 3468 +

 3469 + logger.info( f"Role scan: { len (all_snippets)} articles from { len (scan_states)} states" )

 3470 +

 3471 + combined_text = ""

 3472 + for s in all_snippets:

 3473 + combined_text += f"\n[ {s[ 'searched_state' ]} ] {s[ 'title' ]} \n {s[ 'snippet' ]} \nURL: {s[ 'url' ]} \n"

 3474 + combined_text = combined_text[: 15000 ] # Larger context for more results

 3475 +

 3476 + if progress_callback:

 3477 + progress_callback( f"Extracting role targets from { len (all_snippets)} articles..." )

 3478 +

 3479 + import anthropic

 3480 + try :

 3481 + client = anthropic.Anthropic(timeout= 90.0 )

 3482 + prompt = f"""Extract K-12 school district staff members with CS/CTE/STEM/Technology leadership roles from these search results.

 3483 +

 3484 + INCLUDE only people with these types of titles at K- 12 public school districts:

 3485 + - Directors/Coordinators of: Computer Science, CTE, STEM, STEAM, Technology, EdTech, Curriculum

 3486 + - Specialized teachers: Cybersecurity, Game Design, Esports, Robotics, Computer Science

 3487 + - Any CS/CTE/STEM leadership role at a school district

 3488 +

 3489 + EXCLUDE:

 3490 + - College/university staff

 3491 + - State department of education employees (unless at a district)

 3492 + - IT infrastructure roles (network admin, help desk, sysadmin)

 3493 + - Generic "teacher" or "principal" without CS/STEM specifics

 3494 + - People at non-education organizations

 3495 +

 3496 + For each person, extract:

 3497 + {{

 3498 + "person_name" : "Full name (Dr./Mr./Mrs. if shown)" ,

 3499 + "title" : "Their exact job title" ,

 3500 + "district" : "School district name" ,

 3501 + "state" : "2-letter state code" ,

 3502 + "headline" : "[person_name], [title] at [district] ([state])"

 3503 + }}

 3504 +

 3505 + Return a JSON array. If nothing qualifies, return [].

 3506 + Return ONLY a valid JSON array — no commentary.

 3507 +

 3508 + Search results:

 3509 + {combined_text} """

 3510 +

 3511 + response = client.messages.create(

 3512 + model= "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" ,

 3513 + max_tokens= 3000 ,

 3514 + messages=[{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 3515 + )

 3516 + _track_usage(response)

 3517 + raw_text = response.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 3518 + except Exception as e:

 3519 + logger.error( f"Claude role extraction failed: {e} " )

 3520 + return []

 3521 +

 3522 + try :

 3523 + clean = raw_text

 3524 + if "```" in clean:

 3525 + clean = clean.split( "```" )[ 1 ]

 3526 + if clean.startswith( "json" ):

 3527 + clean = clean[ 4 :]

 3528 + start = clean.find( "[" )

 3529 + end = clean.rfind( "]" )

 3530 + if start == - 1 or end == - 1 :

 3531 + logger.warning( "Role scan: no JSON array in response" )

 3532 + return []

 3533 + items = json.loads(clean[start:end + 1 ])

 3534 + except json.JSONDecodeError as e:

 3535 + logger.warning( f"Role scan JSON parse failed: {e} " )

 3536 + return []

 3537 +

 3538 + signals = []

 3539 + dedup_seen = set ()

 3540 + year_month = datetime.now().strftime( "%Y_%m" )

 3541 +

 3542 + for item in items:

 3543 + person_name = (item.get( "person_name" ) or "" ).strip()

 3544 + title = (item.get( "title" ) or "" ).strip()

 3545 + district = (item.get( "district" ) or "" ).strip()

 3546 + state = (item.get( "state" ) or "" ).strip().upper()

 3547 + if not person_name or not district or not state or len (state) != 2 :

 3548 + continue

 3549 +

 3550 + nces_state = lookup_district_state(district)

 3551 + if nces_state:

 3552 + state = nces_state

 3553 +

 3554 + # Person-level dedup: same person at same district in same month

 3555 + norm_person = csv_importer.normalize_name(person_name)

 3556 + norm_district = csv_importer.normalize_name(district)

 3557 + dedup_key = (norm_person, norm_district, year_month)

 3558 + if dedup_key in dedup_seen:

 3559 + continue

 3560 + dedup_seen.add(dedup_key)

 3561 +

 3562 + in_territory = state in TERRITORY_STATES

 3563 + cust_status = check_customer_status(district)

 3564 + heat = compute_heat_score( "hiring" , 1 , in_territory, cust_status)

 3565 +

 3566 + headline = (item.get( "headline" ) or f" {person_name} , {title} at {district} " ).strip()

 3567 + msg_id = f"role_ {norm_person} _ {norm_district} _ {year_month} "

 3568 +

 3569 + signals.append({

 3570 + "date" : datetime.now().strftime( "%Y-%m-%d" ),

 3571 + "source" : "role_scan" ,

 3572 + "source_detail" : f"Serper — {title[: 40 ]} " ,

 3573 + "signal_type" : "hiring" ,

 3574 + "scope" : "district" ,

 3575 + "district" : district,

 3576 + "state" : state,

 3577 + "headline" : headline[: 200 ],

 3578 + "dollar_amount" : "" ,

 3579 + "tier" : 1 ,

 3580 + "heat_score" : heat,

 3581 + "urgency" : "routine" ,

 3582 + "customer_status" : cust_status,

 3583 + "url" : "" ,

 3584 + "message_id" : msg_id,

 3585 + })

 3586 +

 3587 + logger.info( f"Role scan: { len (signals)} CS/CTE/STEM leaders extracted "

 3588 + f"( { len (items)} raw, { len (items) - len (signals)} filtered/deduped)" )

 3589 + if progress_callback:

 3590 + progress_callback( f"Role scan: { len (signals)} targets found" )

 3591 + return signals

 3592 +

 3593 +

 3594 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 3595 + # CSTA CHAPTER PROSPECTING (#8) — Serper + Claude

 3596 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 3597 +

 3598 + def scan_csta_chapters ( states= None , progress_callback= None ) -> list :

 3599 + """

 3600 + Scan for CSTA chapter leaders and active members in territory states.

 3601 + Uses Serper + Claude Haiku. Returns list of signal dicts.

 3602 + Cost: ~$ 1.20 /scan ( 24 Serper queries + 1 Claude call).

 3603 + """

 3604 + if not SERPER_API_KEY:

 3605 + logger.warning( "SERPER_API_KEY not set — cannot scan CSTA chapters" )

 3606 + return []

 3607 +

 3608 + import httpx

 3609 +

 3610 + _load_nces_lookup()

 3611 + _load_cross_references()

 3612 +

 3613 + scan_states = states or list (TERRITORY_STATES)

 3614 + all_snippets = []

 3615 + seen_urls = set ()

 3616 +

 3617 + if progress_callback:

 3618 + progress_callback( f"Searching { len (scan_states)} states for CSTA chapter leaders..." )

 3619 +

 3620 + for state_abbr in scan_states:

 3621 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_abbr, state_abbr)

 3622 + queries = [

 3623 + f'"CSTA" chapter " {state_name} " leader OR president OR board member school district' ,

 3624 + f'"computer science teachers association" " {state_name} " conference OR workshop OR event school' ,

 3625 + ]

 3626 + for query in queries:

 3627 + try :

 3628 + resp = httpx.post(

 3629 + SERPER_URL,

 3630 + headers={ "X-API-Key" : SERPER_API_KEY, "Content-Type" : "application/json" },

 3631 + json={ "q" : query, "num" : 10 , "tbs" : "qdr:y" },

 3632 + timeout= 15.0 ,

 3633 + )

 3634 + data = resp.json()

 3635 + for item in data.get( "organic" , [])[: 10 ]:

 3636 + url = item.get( "link" , "" )

 3637 + if url and url not in seen_urls:

 3638 + seen_urls.add(url)

 3639 + all_snippets.append({

 3640 + "title" : item.get( "title" , "" ),

 3641 + "snippet" : item.get( "snippet" , "" ),

 3642 + "url" : url,

 3643 + "searched_state" : state_abbr,

 3644 + })

 3645 + time.sleep( 0.5 )

 3646 + except Exception as e:

 3647 + logger.warning( f"Serper CSTA scan failed for {state_abbr} : {e} " )

 3648 +

 3649 + if not all_snippets:

 3650 + logger.info( "CSTA scan: no results found" )

 3651 + if progress_callback:

 3652 + progress_callback( "CSTA scan: no results found." )

 3653 + return []

 3654 +

 3655 + logger.info( f"CSTA scan: { len (all_snippets)} articles from { len (scan_states)} states" )

 3656 +

 3657 + combined_text = ""

 3658 + for s in all_snippets:

 3659 + combined_text += f"\n[ {s[ 'searched_state' ]} ] {s[ 'title' ]} \n {s[ 'snippet' ]} \nURL: {s[ 'url' ]} \n"

 3660 + combined_text = combined_text[: 12000 ]

 3661 +

 3662 + if progress_callback:

 3663 + progress_callback( f"Extracting CSTA members from { len (all_snippets)} articles..." )

 3664 +

 3665 + import anthropic

 3666 + try :

 3667 + client = anthropic.Anthropic(timeout= 90.0 )

 3668 + prompt = f"""Extract CSTA (Computer Science Teachers Association) chapter leaders, board members, conference speakers, and active K-12 CS educators from these search results.

 3669 +

 3670 + INCLUDE:

 3671 + - CSTA state/regional chapter presidents, vice presidents, board members

 3672 + - CSTA conference speakers/presenters who work at K- 12 school districts

 3673 + - Active CSTA members mentioned alongside their school district

 3674 +

 3675 + EXCLUDE:

 3676 + - University/college professors

 3677 + - People at non-education organizations

 3678 + - Generic CSTA news without specific people

 3679 + - National CSTA staff ( not district-level)

 3680 +

 3681 + For each person:

 3682 + {{

 3683 + "person_name" : "Full name" ,

 3684 + "role" : "CSTA chapter role or school title" ,

 3685 + "district" : "School district name (if mentioned)" ,

 3686 + "state" : "2-letter state code" ,

 3687 + "headline" : "[person_name], [role] — CSTA [state] chapter"

 3688 + }}

 3689 +

 3690 + Return a JSON array. If nothing qualifies, return [].

 3691 + Return ONLY valid JSON — no commentary.

 3692 +

 3693 + Search results:

 3694 + {combined_text} """

 3695 +

 3696 + response = client.messages.create(

 3697 + model= "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" ,

 3698 + max_tokens= 2000 ,

 3699 + messages=[{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 3700 + )

 3701 + _track_usage(response)

 3702 + raw_text = response.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 3703 + except Exception as e:

 3704 + logger.error( f"Claude CSTA extraction failed: {e} " )

 3705 + return []

 3706 +

 3707 + try :

 3708 + clean = raw_text

 3709 + if "```" in clean:

 3710 + clean = clean.split( "```" )[ 1 ]

 3711 + if clean.startswith( "json" ):

 3712 + clean = clean[ 4 :]

 3713 + start = clean.find( "[" )

 3714 + end = clean.rfind( "]" )

 3715 + if start == - 1 or end == - 1 :

 3716 + return []

 3717 + items = json.loads(clean[start:end + 1 ])

 3718 + except json.JSONDecodeError:

 3719 + return []

 3720 +

 3721 + signals = []

 3722 + dedup_seen = set ()

 3723 + year_month = datetime.now().strftime( "%Y_%m" )

 3724 +

 3725 + for item in items:

 3726 + person_name = (item.get( "person_name" ) or "" ).strip()

 3727 + state = (item.get( "state" ) or "" ).strip().upper()

 3728 + district = (item.get( "district" ) or "" ).strip()

 3729 + if not person_name or not state or len (state) != 2 :

 3730 + continue

 3731 +

 3732 + norm_person = csv_importer.normalize_name(person_name)

 3733 + dedup_key = (norm_person, state, year_month)

 3734 + if dedup_key in dedup_seen:

 3735 + continue

 3736 + dedup_seen.add(dedup_key)

 3737 +

 3738 + in_territory = state in TERRITORY_STATES

 3739 + cust_status = check_customer_status(district) if district else "new"

 3740 + heat = compute_heat_score( "hiring" , 2 , in_territory, cust_status)

 3741 +

 3742 + headline = (item.get( "headline" ) or f" {person_name} , CSTA {state} " ).strip()

 3743 + msg_id = f"csta_ {norm_person} _ {state} _ {year_month} "

 3744 +

 3745 + signals.append({

 3746 + "date" : datetime.now().strftime( "%Y-%m-%d" ),

 3747 + "source" : "csta_scan" ,

 3748 + "source_detail" : f"Serper — CSTA {state} " ,

 3749 + "signal_type" : "hiring" ,

 3750 + "scope" : "district" if district else "state" ,

 3751 + "district" : district,

 3752 + "state" : state,

 3753 + "headline" : headline[: 200 ],

 3754 + "dollar_amount" : "" ,

 3755 + "tier" : 2 ,

 3756 + "heat_score" : heat,

 3757 + "urgency" : "routine" ,

 3758 + "customer_status" : cust_status,

 3759 + "url" : "" ,

 3760 + "message_id" : msg_id,

 3761 + })

 3762 +

 3763 + logger.info( f"CSTA scan: { len (signals)} chapter leaders/members extracted" )

 3764 + if progress_callback:

 3765 + progress_callback( f"CSTA scan: { len (signals)} targets found" )

 3766 + return signals

 3767 +

 3768 +

 3769 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 3770 # RSS FEED INGESTION

 3771 # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 636 +

 637 +

 638 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 639 + # DORMANT LEAD DETECTION (#12)

 640 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 641 +

 642 + def get_dormant_accounts ( days: int = 90 ) -> list [ dict ]:

 643 + """

 644 + Find accounts with Scout-tracked activity that went silent.

 645 + Only returns accounts that HAVE had activity (at least 1 logged event)

 646 + but whose most recent activity is >= `days` ago.

 647 + Excludes current active customers.

 648 +

 649 + Returns list of dicts sorted by days_dormant descending:

 650 + [{account, last_date, last_type, days_dormant, activity_count}]

 651 + """

 652 + from datetime import datetime as dt

 653 +

 654 + try :

 655 + all_rows = _load_activity_rows() # No date filter — all history

 656 + if not all_rows:

 657 + return []

 658 +

 659 + # Group by account

 660 + by_account = {}

 661 + for row in all_rows:

 662 + account = (row.get( "District/Account" ) or "" ).strip()

 663 + if not account:

 664 + continue

 665 + date_str = row.get( "Date" , "" )

 666 + act_type = row.get( "Type" , "" )

 667 + if account not in by_account:

 668 + by_account[account] = { "dates" : [], "types" : [], "count" : 0 }

 669 + by_account[account][ "dates" ].append(date_str)

 670 + by_account[account][ "types" ].append(act_type)

 671 + by_account[account][ "count" ] += 1

 672 +

 673 + # Build exclusion set from active customers

 674 + try :

 675 + import tools.csv_importer as csv_importer

 676 + active_accounts = csv_importer.get_active_accounts()

 677 + active_keys = set ()

 678 + for acc in active_accounts:

 679 + name = (acc.get( "Active Account Name" , "" ) or acc.get( "Display Name" , "" )).strip()

 680 + if name:

 681 + active_keys.add(name.lower())

 682 + active_keys.add(csv_importer.normalize_name(name))

 683 + except Exception:

 684 + active_keys = set ()

 685 +

 686 + today = dt.now().date()

 687 + dormant = []

 688 +

 689 + for account, data in by_account.items():

 690 + # Skip active customers

 691 + if account.lower() in active_keys or csv_importer.normalize_name(account) in active_keys:

 692 + continue

 693 +

 694 + # Find most recent activity date

 695 + latest_date = None

 696 + latest_type = ""

 697 + for i, d in enumerate (data[ "dates" ]):

 698 + try :

 699 + parsed = dt.strptime(d, "%Y-%m-%d" ).date()

 700 + if latest_date is None or parsed > latest_date:

 701 + latest_date = parsed

 702 + latest_type = data[ "types" ][i]

 703 + except (Value Error, Type Error):

 704 + continue

 705 +

 706 + if latest_date is None :

 707 + continue

 708 +

 709 + days_dormant = (today - latest_date).days

 710 + if days_dormant >= days:

 711 + dormant.append({

 712 + "account" : account,

 713 + "last_date" : latest_date.strftime( "%Y-%m-%d" ),

 714 + "last_type" : latest_type,

 715 + "days_dormant" : days_dormant,

 716 + "activity_count" : data[ "count" ],

 717 + })

 718 +

 719 + dormant.sort(key= lambda x: x[ "days_dormant" ], reverse= True )

 720 + return dormant

 721 +

 722 + except Exception as e:

 723 + logger.error( f"get_dormant_accounts error: {e} " , exc_info= True )

 724 + return []

 725 +

 726 +

 727 + def format_dormant_for_telegram ( dormant: list , limit: int = 20 ) -> str :

 728 + """Format dormant accounts for Telegram display."""

 729 + if not dormant:

 730 + return "No dormant accounts found."

 731 +

 732 + lines = [ f"💤 *Dormant Accounts* ( { len (dormant)} total)\n" ]

 733 + for i, d in enumerate (dormant[:limit], 1 ):

 734 + lines.append(

 735 + f" {i} . * {d[ 'account' ]} *\n"

 736 + f" Last: {d[ 'last_date' ]} ( {d[ 'days_dormant' ]} d ago) — {d[ 'last_type' ]} "

 737 + f" | {d[ 'activity_count' ]} total activities"

 738 + )

 739 + if len (dormant) > limit:

 740 + lines.append( f"\n ... and { len (dormant) - limit} more" )

 741 + return "\n" .join(lines)

 479 ---

 481 + ---

 482 +

 483 + ## ARCHETYPE: Pilot Offer

 484 +

 485 + **Target:** Any district/school contact considering CodeCombat — warm leads, signal-driven prospects,

 486 + conference contacts, webinar attendees, or districts in evaluation mode.

 487 +

 488 + **Core angle:** Zero-risk pilot — give CodeCombat a real test drive with your students and teachers

 489 + before committing. No budget needed, no contracts, no obligations.

 490 +

 491 + **Sequence structure (4 steps, 12 days):**

 492 +

 493 + | Step | Day | Label | Goal |

 494 + |------|-----|-------|------|

 495 + | 1 | 0 | Pilot invite | Offer the pilot: X licenses, Y weeks, zero commitment. Lead with what they get, not what it costs. Under 80 words. CTA: "Would a free pilot be useful for your team?" |

 496 + | 2 | 3 | Social proof | Pattern framing: "Most educators I work with find that students are writing real Python code by the end of week one." Reference engagement data (99% teacher satisfactio

 + n, 850K+ lines of code per student). |

 497 + | 3 | 7 | Implementation ease | Zero prep required — no coding background needed for teachers (2/3 of successful teachers started with zero experience). Turn-key slides, embedded guidance, auto

 + -grading. Address the "sounds like a lot of work" objection. |

 498 + | 4 | 12 | Deadline + booking | Friendly urgency: pilot spots are limited for this term. Direct Zoom booking link. Short — under 50 words. |

 499 +

 500 + **Tone:** Peer-to-peer, not salesy. Steven is offering something genuinely useful, not asking for a favor.

 501 + Frame as "let your students try it" not "let me demo it for you." The pilot sells itself through student engagement.

 502 +

 503 + **Pilot details:** Steven provides specific pilot terms (license count, duration, start date) when building.

 504 + If not specified, default to "30 licenses for 6 weeks."

 505 +

 506 + ---

 507 +

 508 # A/B TESTING GUIDANCE

 510 When building a sequence, Scout can generate A/B variants. Best candidates:

 ⏺ Bash (git add tools/district_prospector.py agent/main.py && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'

 Block 3: Unresponsive sequence re-engagement (#11)…)

 ⎿   [main 0838466] Block 3: Unresponsive sequence re-engagement (#11)

 ⏺ Block 4: Role/Title-Based Prospecting (#7) — Serper for rare titles only.

 ⏺ Update ( tools/signal_processor.py )

 ⎿   Added 365 lines

 3404 # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 3405 + # ROLE/TITLE-BASED PROSPECTING (#7) — Serper + Claude

 3406 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 3407 +

 3408 + def scan_role_targets ( states= None , progress_callback= None ) -> list :

 3409 + """

 3410 + Scan for K- 12 contacts with specific CS/CTE/STEM leadership titles.

 3411 + Only searches rare/specific titles — common titles (Principal, Teacher)

 3412 + are too noisy for web search and are handled by job posting scanner.

 3413 + Uses Serper + Claude Haiku. Returns list of signal dicts.

 3414 + Cost: ~$ 2.50 /scan ( 48 Serper queries + 1 Claude call).

 3415 + """

 3416 + if not SERPER_API_KEY:

 3417 + logger.warning( "SERPER_API_KEY not set — cannot scan role targets" )

 3418 + return []

 3419 +

 3420 + import httpx

 3421 +

 3422 + _load_nces_lookup()

 3423 + _load_cross_references()

 3424 +

 3425 + scan_states = states or list (TERRITORY_STATES)

 3426 + all_snippets = []

 3427 + seen_urls = set ()

 3428 +

 3429 + if progress_callback:

 3430 + progress_callback( f"Searching { len (scan_states)} states for CS/CTE/STEM leaders (~$2.50)..." )

 3431 +

 3432 + for state_abbr in scan_states:

 3433 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_abbr, state_abbr)

 3434 + queries = [

 3435 + f'"Director of Computer Science" OR "CS Director" OR "CTE Director" OR "Director of CTE" OR "STEM Director" OR "Director of STEM" school district " {state_name} "' ,

 3436 + f'"Director of Technology" OR "EdTech Director" OR "Instructional Technology Director" OR "Educational Technology Director" school district " {state_name} "' ,

 3437 + f'"Curriculum Director" OR "Director of Curriculum" OR "STEM Coordinator" OR "CTE Coordinator" school district " {state_name} "' ,

 3438 + f'"Cybersecurity Teacher" OR "Game Design Teacher" OR "Esports Coach" OR "Robotics Teacher" school district " {state_name} "' ,

 3439 + ]

 3440 + for query in queries:

 3441 + try :

 3442 + resp = httpx.post(

 3443 + SERPER_URL,

 3444 + headers={ "X-API-Key" : SERPER_API_KEY, "Content-Type" : "application/json" },

 3445 + json={ "q" : query, "num" : 10 , "tbs" : "qdr:y" }, # Last year — roles persist

 3446 + timeout= 15.0 ,

 3447 + )

 3448 + data = resp.json()

 3449 + for item in data.get( "organic" , [])[: 10 ]:

 3450 + url = item.get( "link" , "" )

 3451 + if url and url not in seen_urls:

 3452 + seen_urls.add(url)

 3453 + all_snippets.append({

 3454 + "title" : item.get( "title" , "" ),

 3455 + "snippet" : item.get( "snippet" , "" ),

 3456 + "url" : url,

 3457 + "searched_state" : state_abbr,

 3458 + })

 3459 + time.sleep( 0.5 )

 3460 + except Exception as e:

 3461 + logger.warning( f"Serper role scan failed for {state_abbr} : {e} " )

 3462 +

 3463 + if not all_snippets:

 3464 + logger.info( "Role scan: no results found" )

 3465 + if progress_callback:

 3466 + progress_callback( "Role scan: no results found." )

 3467 + return []

 3468 +

 3469 + logger.info( f"Role scan: { len (all_snippets)} articles from { len (scan_states)} states" )

 3470 +

 3471 + combined_text = ""

 3472 + for s in all_snippets:

 3473 + combined_text += f"\n[ {s[ 'searched_state' ]} ] {s[ 'title' ]} \n {s[ 'snippet' ]} \nURL: {s[ 'url' ]} \n"

 3474 + combined_text = combined_text[: 15000 ] # Larger context for more results

 3475 +

 3476 + if progress_callback:

 3477 + progress_callback( f"Extracting role targets from { len (all_snippets)} articles..." )

 3478 +

 3479 + import anthropic

 3480 + try :

 3481 + client = anthropic.Anthropic(timeout= 90.0 )

 3482 + prompt = f"""Extract K-12 school district staff members with CS/CTE/STEM/Technology leadership roles from these search results.

 3483 +

 3484 + INCLUDE only people with these types of titles at K- 12 public school districts:

 3485 + - Directors/Coordinators of: Computer Science, CTE, STEM, STEAM, Technology, EdTech, Curriculum

 3486 + - Specialized teachers: Cybersecurity, Game Design, Esports, Robotics, Computer Science

 3487 + - Any CS/CTE/STEM leadership role at a school district

 3488 +

 3489 + EXCLUDE:

 3490 + - College/university staff

 3491 + - State department of education employees (unless at a district)

 3492 + - IT infrastructure roles (network admin, help desk, sysadmin)

 3493 + - Generic "teacher" or "principal" without CS/STEM specifics

 3494 + - People at non-education organizations

 3495 +

 3496 + For each person, extract:

 3497 + {{

 3498 + "person_name" : "Full name (Dr./Mr./Mrs. if shown)" ,

 3499 + "title" : "Their exact job title" ,

 3500 + "district" : "School district name" ,

 3501 + "state" : "2-letter state code" ,

 3502 + "headline" : "[person_name], [title] at [district] ([state])"

 3503 + }}

 3504 +

 3505 + Return a JSON array. If nothing qualifies, return [].

 3506 + Return ONLY a valid JSON array — no commentary.

 3507 +

 3508 + Search results:

 3509 + {combined_text} """

 3510 +

 3511 + response = client.messages.create(

 3512 + model= "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" ,

 3513 + max_tokens= 3000 ,

 3514 + messages=[{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 3515 + )

 3516 + _track_usage(response)

 3517 + raw_text = response.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 3518 + except Exception as e:

 3519 + logger.error( f"Claude role extraction failed: {e} " )

 3520 + return []

 3521 +

 3522 + try :

 3523 + clean = raw_text

 3524 + if "```" in clean:

 3525 + clean = clean.split( "```" )[ 1 ]

 3526 + if clean.startswith( "json" ):

 3527 + clean = clean[ 4 :]

 3528 + start = clean.find( "[" )

 3529 + end = clean.rfind( "]" )

 3530 + if start == - 1 or end == - 1 :

 3531 + logger.warning( "Role scan: no JSON array in response" )

 3532 + return []

 3533 + items = json.loads(clean[start:end + 1 ])

 3534 + except json.JSONDecodeError as e:

 3535 + logger.warning( f"Role scan JSON parse failed: {e} " )

 3536 + return []

 3537 +

 3538 + signals = []

 3539 + dedup_seen = set ()

 3540 + year_month = datetime.now().strftime( "%Y_%m" )

 3541 +

 3542 + for item in items:

 3543 + person_name = (item.get( "person_name" ) or "" ).strip()

 3544 + title = (item.get( "title" ) or "" ).strip()

 3545 + district = (item.get( "district" ) or "" ).strip()

 3546 + state = (item.get( "state" ) or "" ).strip().upper()

 3547 + if not person_name or not district or not state or len (state) != 2 :

 3548 + continue

 3549 +

 3550 + nces_state = lookup_district_state(district)

 3551 + if nces_state:

 3552 + state = nces_state

 3553 +

 3554 + # Person-level dedup: same person at same district in same month

 3555 + norm_person = csv_importer.normalize_name(person_name)

 3556 + norm_district = csv_importer.normalize_name(district)

 3557 + dedup_key = (norm_person, norm_district, year_month)

 3558 + if dedup_key in dedup_seen:

 3559 + continue

 3560 + dedup_seen.add(dedup_key)

 3561 +

 3562 + in_territory = state in TERRITORY_STATES

 3563 + cust_status = check_customer_status(district)

 3564 + heat = compute_heat_score( "hiring" , 1 , in_territory, cust_status)

 3565 +

 3566 + headline = (item.get( "headline" ) or f" {person_name} , {title} at {district} " ).strip()

 3567 + msg_id = f"role_ {norm_person} _ {norm_district} _ {year_month} "

 3568 +

 3569 + signals.append({

 3570 + "date" : datetime.now().strftime( "%Y-%m-%d" ),

 3571 + "source" : "role_scan" ,

 3572 + "source_detail" : f"Serper — {title[: 40 ]} " ,

 3573 + "signal_type" : "hiring" ,

 3574 + "scope" : "district" ,

 3575 + "district" : district,

 3576 + "state" : state,

 3577 + "headline" : headline[: 200 ],

 3578 + "dollar_amount" : "" ,

 3579 + "tier" : 1 ,

 3580 + "heat_score" : heat,

 3581 + "urgency" : "routine" ,

 3582 + "customer_status" : cust_status,

 3583 + "url" : "" ,

 3584 + "message_id" : msg_id,

 3585 + })

 3586 +

 3587 + logger.info( f"Role scan: { len (signals)} CS/CTE/STEM leaders extracted "

 3588 + f"( { len (items)} raw, { len (items) - len (signals)} filtered/deduped)" )

 3589 + if progress_callback:

 3590 + progress_callback( f"Role scan: { len (signals)} targets found" )

 3591 + return signals

 3592 +

 3593 +

 3594 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 3595 + # CSTA CHAPTER PROSPECTING (#8) — Serper + Claude

 3596 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 3597 +

 3598 + def scan_csta_chapters ( states= None , progress_callback= None ) -> list :

 3599 + """

 3600 + Scan for CSTA chapter leaders and active members in territory states.

 3601 + Uses Serper + Claude Haiku. Returns list of signal dicts.

 3602 + Cost: ~$ 1.20 /scan ( 24 Serper queries + 1 Claude call).

 3603 + """

 3604 + if not SERPER_API_KEY:

 3605 + logger.warning( "SERPER_API_KEY not set — cannot scan CSTA chapters" )

 3606 + return []

 3607 +

 3608 + import httpx

 3609 +

 3610 + _load_nces_lookup()

 3611 + _load_cross_references()

 3612 +

 3613 + scan_states = states or list (TERRITORY_STATES)

 3614 + all_snippets = []

 3615 + seen_urls = set ()

 3616 +

 3617 + if progress_callback:

 3618 + progress_callback( f"Searching { len (scan_states)} states for CSTA chapter leaders..." )

 3619 +

 3620 + for state_abbr in scan_states:

 3621 + state_name = ABBR_TO_STATE_NAME.get(state_abbr, state_abbr)

 3622 + queries = [

 3623 + f'"CSTA" chapter " {state_name} " leader OR president OR board member school district' ,

 3624 + f'"computer science teachers association" " {state_name} " conference OR workshop OR event school' ,

 3625 + ]

 3626 + for query in queries:

 3627 + try :

 3628 + resp = httpx.post(

 3629 + SERPER_URL,

 3630 + headers={ "X-API-Key" : SERPER_API_KEY, "Content-Type" : "application/json" },

 3631 + json={ "q" : query, "num" : 10 , "tbs" : "qdr:y" },

 3632 + timeout= 15.0 ,

 3633 + )

 3634 + data = resp.json()

 3635 + for item in data.get( "organic" , [])[: 10 ]:

 3636 + url = item.get( "link" , "" )

 3637 + if url and url not in seen_urls:

 3638 + seen_urls.add(url)

 3639 + all_snippets.append({

 3640 + "title" : item.get( "title" , "" ),

 3641 + "snippet" : item.get( "snippet" , "" ),

 3642 + "url" : url,

 3643 + "searched_state" : state_abbr,

 3644 + })

 3645 + time.sleep( 0.5 )

 3646 + except Exception as e:

 3647 + logger.warning( f"Serper CSTA scan failed for {state_abbr} : {e} " )

 3648 +

 3649 + if not all_snippets:

 3650 + logger.info( "CSTA scan: no results found" )

 3651 + if progress_callback:

 3652 + progress_callback( "CSTA scan: no results found." )

 3653 + return []

 3654 +

 3655 + logger.info( f"CSTA scan: { len (all_snippets)} articles from { len (scan_states)} states" )

 3656 +

 3657 + combined_text = ""

 3658 + for s in all_snippets:

 3659 + combined_text += f"\n[ {s[ 'searched_state' ]} ] {s[ 'title' ]} \n {s[ 'snippet' ]} \nURL: {s[ 'url' ]} \n"

 3660 + combined_text = combined_text[: 12000 ]

 3661 +

 3662 + if progress_callback:

 3663 + progress_callback( f"Extracting CSTA members from { len (all_snippets)} articles..." )

 3664 +

 3665 + import anthropic

 3666 + try :

 3667 + client = anthropic.Anthropic(timeout= 90.0 )

 3668 + prompt = f"""Extract CSTA (Computer Science Teachers Association) chapter leaders, board members, conference speakers, and active K-12 CS educators from these search results.

 3669 +

 3670 + INCLUDE:

 3671 + - CSTA state/regional chapter presidents, vice presidents, board members

 3672 + - CSTA conference speakers/presenters who work at K- 12 school districts

 3673 + - Active CSTA members mentioned alongside their school district

 3674 +

 3675 + EXCLUDE:

 3676 + - University/college professors

 3677 + - People at non-education organizations

 3678 + - Generic CSTA news without specific people

 3679 + - National CSTA staff ( not district-level)

 3680 +

 3681 + For each person:

 3682 + {{

 3683 + "person_name" : "Full name" ,

 3684 + "role" : "CSTA chapter role or school title" ,

 3685 + "district" : "School district name (if mentioned)" ,

 3686 + "state" : "2-letter state code" ,

 3687 + "headline" : "[person_name], [role] — CSTA [state] chapter"

 3688 + }}

 3689 +

 3690 + Return a JSON array. If nothing qualifies, return [].

 3691 + Return ONLY valid JSON — no commentary.

 3692 +

 3693 + Search results:

 3694 + {combined_text} """

 3695 +

 3696 + response = client.messages.create(

 3697 + model= "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" ,

 3698 + max_tokens= 2000 ,

 3699 + messages=[{ "role" : "user" , "content" : prompt}],

 3700 + )

 3701 + _track_usage(response)

 3702 + raw_text = response.content[ 0 ].text.strip()

 3703 + except Exception as e:

 3704 + logger.error( f"Claude CSTA extraction failed: {e} " )

 3705 + return []

 3706 +

 3707 + try :

 3708 + clean = raw_text

 3709 + if "```" in clean:

 3710 + clean = clean.split( "```" )[ 1 ]

 3711 + if clean.startswith( "json" ):

 3712 + clean = clean[ 4 :]

 3713 + start = clean.find( "[" )

 3714 + end = clean.rfind( "]" )

 3715 + if start == - 1 or end == - 1 :

 3716 + return []

 3717 + items = json.loads(clean[start:end + 1 ])

 3718 + except json.JSONDecodeError:

 3719 + return []

 3720 +

 3721 + signals = []

 3722 + dedup_seen = set ()

 3723 + year_month = datetime.now().strftime( "%Y_%m" )

 3724 +

 3725 + for item in items:

 3726 + person_name = (item.get( "person_name" ) or "" ).strip()

 3727 + state = (item.get( "state" ) or "" ).strip().upper()

 3728 + district = (item.get( "district" ) or "" ).strip()

 3729 + if not person_name or not state or len (state) != 2 :

 3730 + continue

 3731 +

 3732 + norm_person = csv_importer.normalize_name(person_name)

 3733 + dedup_key = (norm_person, state, year_month)

 3734 + if dedup_key in dedup_seen:

 3735 + continue

 3736 + dedup_seen.add(dedup_key)

 3737 +

 3738 + in_territory = state in TERRITORY_STATES

 3739 + cust_status = check_customer_status(district) if district else "new"

 3740 + heat = compute_heat_score( "hiring" , 2 , in_territory, cust_status)

 3741 +

 3742 + headline = (item.get( "headline" ) or f" {person_name} , CSTA {state} " ).strip()

 3743 + msg_id = f"csta_ {norm_person} _ {state} _ {year_month} "

 3744 +

 3745 + signals.append({

 3746 + "date" : datetime.now().strftime( "%Y-%m-%d" ),

 3747 + "source" : "csta_scan" ,

 3748 + "source_detail" : f"Serper — CSTA {state} " ,

 3749 + "signal_type" : "hiring" ,

 3750 + "scope" : "district" if district else "state" ,

 3751 + "district" : district,

 3752 + "state" : state,

 3753 + "headline" : headline[: 200 ],

 3754 + "dollar_amount" : "" ,

 3755 + "tier" : 2 ,

 3756 + "heat_score" : heat,

 3757 + "urgency" : "routine" ,

 3758 + "customer_status" : cust_status,

 3759 + "url" : "" ,

 3760 + "message_id" : msg_id,

 3761 + })

 3762 +

 3763 + logger.info( f"CSTA scan: { len (signals)} chapter leaders/members extracted" )

 3764 + if progress_callback:

 3765 + progress_callback( f"CSTA scan: { len (signals)} targets found" )

 3766 + return signals

 3767 +

 3768 +

 3769 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 3770 # RSS FEED INGESTION

 3771 # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 ⎿   Added 106 lines

 633 lines.append( f" {label} : {d[ 'actual' ]} / {d[ 'target' ]} ( {d[ 'pct' ]} %)" )

 635 return "\n" .join(lines)

 636 +

 637 +

 638 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 639 + # DORMANT LEAD DETECTION (#12)

 640 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 641 +

 642 + def get_dormant_accounts ( days: int = 90 ) -> list [ dict ]:

 643 + """

 644 + Find accounts with Scout-tracked activity that went silent.

 645 + Only returns accounts that HAVE had activity (at least 1 logged event)

 646 + but whose most recent activity is >= `days` ago.

 647 + Excludes current active customers.

 648 +

 649 + Returns list of dicts sorted by days_dormant descending:

 650 + [{account, last_date, last_type, days_dormant, activity_count}]

 651 + """

 652 + from datetime import datetime as dt

 653 +

 654 + try :

 655 + all_rows = _load_activity_rows() # No date filter — all history

 656 + if not all_rows:

 657 + return []

 658 +

 659 + # Group by account

 660 + by_account = {}

 661 + for row in all_rows:

 662 + account = (row.get( "District/Account" ) or "" ).strip()

 663 + if not account:

 664 + continue

 665 + date_str = row.get( "Date" , "" )

 666 + act_type = row.get( "Type" , "" )

 667 + if account not in by_account:

 668 + by_account[account] = { "dates" : [], "types" : [], "count" : 0 }

 669 + by_account[account][ "dates" ].append(date_str)

 670 + by_account[account][ "types" ].append(act_type)

 671 + by_account[account][ "count" ] += 1

 672 +

 673 + # Build exclusion set from active customers

 674 + try :

 675 + import tools.csv_importer as csv_importer

 676 + active_accounts = csv_importer.get_active_accounts()

 677 + active_keys = set ()

 678 + for acc in active_accounts:

 679 + name = (acc.get( "Active Account Name" , "" ) or acc.get( "Display Name" , "" )).strip()

 680 + if name:

 681 + active_keys.add(name.lower())

 682 + active_keys.add(csv_importer.normalize_name(name))

 683 + except Exception:

 684 + active_keys = set ()

 685 +

 686 + today = dt.now().date()

 687 + dormant = []

 688 +

 689 + for account, data in by_account.items():

 690 + # Skip active customers

 691 + if account.lower() in active_keys or csv_importer.normalize_name(account) in active_keys:

 692 + continue

 693 +

 694 + # Find most recent activity date

 695 + latest_date = None

 696 + latest_type = ""

 697 + for i, d in enumerate (data[ "dates" ]):

 698 + try :

 699 + parsed = dt.strptime(d, "%Y-%m-%d" ).date()

 700 + if latest_date is None or parsed > latest_date:

 701 + latest_date = parsed

 702 + latest_type = data[ "types" ][i]

 703 + except (Value Error, Type Error):

 704 + continue

 705 +

 706 + if latest_date is None :

 707 + continue

 708 +

 709 + days_dormant = (today - latest_date).days

 710 + if days_dormant >= days:

 711 + dormant.append({

 712 + "account" : account,

 713 + "last_date" : latest_date.strftime( "%Y-%m-%d" ),

 714 + "last_type" : latest_type,

 715 + "days_dormant" : days_dormant,

 716 + "activity_count" : data[ "count" ],

 717 + })

 718 +

 719 + dormant.sort(key= lambda x: x[ "days_dormant" ], reverse= True )

 720 + return dormant

 721 +

 722 + except Exception as e:

 723 + logger.error( f"get_dormant_accounts error: {e} " , exc_info= True )

 724 + return []

 725 +

 726 +

 727 + def format_dormant_for_telegram ( dormant: list , limit: int = 20 ) -> str :

 728 + """Format dormant accounts for Telegram display."""

 729 + if not dormant:

 730 + return "No dormant accounts found."

 731 +

 732 + lines = [ f"💤 *Dormant Accounts* ( { len (dormant)} total)\n" ]

 733 + for i, d in enumerate (dormant[:limit], 1 ):

 734 + lines.append(

 735 + f" {i} . * {d[ 'account' ]} *\n"

 736 + f" Last: {d[ 'last_date' ]} ( {d[ 'days_dormant' ]} d ago) — {d[ 'last_type' ]} "

 737 + f" | {d[ 'activity_count' ]} total activities"

 738 + )

 739 + if len (dormant) > limit:

 740 + lines.append( f"\n ... and { len (dormant) - limit} more" )

 741 + return "\n" .join(lines)

 ⎿   Added 27 lines

 479 ---

 481 + ---

 482 +

 483 + ## ARCHETYPE: Pilot Offer

 484 +

 485 + **Target:** Any district/school contact considering CodeCombat — warm leads, signal-driven prospects,

 486 + conference contacts, webinar attendees, or districts in evaluation mode.

 487 +

 488 + **Core angle:** Zero-risk pilot — give CodeCombat a real test drive with your students and teachers

 489 + before committing. No budget needed, no contracts, no obligations.

 490 +

 491 + **Sequence structure (4 steps, 12 days):**

 492 +

 493 + | Step | Day | Label | Goal |

 494 + |------|-----|-------|------|

 495 + | 1 | 0 | Pilot invite | Offer the pilot: X licenses, Y weeks, zero commitment. Lead with what they get, not what it costs. Under 80 words. CTA: "Would a free pilot be useful for your team?" |

 496 + | 2 | 3 | Social proof | Pattern framing: "Most educators I work with find that students are writing real Python code by the end of week one." Reference engagement data (99% teacher satisfactio

 + n, 850K+ lines of code per student). |

 497 + | 3 | 7 | Implementation ease | Zero prep required — no coding background needed for teachers (2/3 of successful teachers started with zero experience). Turn-key slides, embedded guidance, auto

 + -grading. Address the "sounds like a lot of work" objection. |

 498 + | 4 | 12 | Deadline + booking | Friendly urgency: pilot spots are limited for this term. Direct Zoom booking link. Short — under 50 words. |

 499 +

 500 + **Tone:** Peer-to-peer, not salesy. Steven is offering something genuinely useful, not asking for a favor.

 501 + Frame as "let your students try it" not "let me demo it for you." The pilot sells itself through student engagement.

 502 +

 503 + **Pilot details:** Steven provides specific pilot terms (license count, duration, start date) when building.

 504 + If not specified, default to "30 licenses for 6 weeks."

 505 +

 506 + ---

 507 +

 508 # A/B TESTING GUIDANCE

 510 When building a sequence, Scout can generate A/B variants. Best candidates:

 ⎿   Added 106 lines

 633 lines.append( f" {label} : {d[ 'actual' ]} / {d[ 'target' ]} ( {d[ 'pct' ]} %)" )

 635 return "\n" .join(lines)

 636 +

 637 +

 638 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 639 + # DORMANT LEAD DETECTION (#12)

 640 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 641 +

 642 + def get_dormant_accounts ( days: int = 90 ) -> list [ dict ]:

 643 + """

 644 + Find accounts with Scout-tracked activity that went silent.

 645 + Only returns accounts that HAVE had activity (at least 1 logged event)

 646 + but whose most recent activity is >= `days` ago.

 647 + Excludes current active customers.

 648 +

 649 + Returns list of dicts sorted by days_dormant descending:

 650 + [{account, last_date, last_type, days_dormant, activity_count}]

 651 + """

 652 + from datetime import datetime as dt

 653 +

 654 + try :

 655 + all_rows = _load_activity_rows() # No date filter — all history

 656 + if not all_rows:

 657 + return []

 658 +

 659 + # Group by account

 660 + by_account = {}

 661 + for row in all_rows:

 662 + account = (row.get( "District/Account" ) or "" ).strip()

 663 + if not account:

 664 + continue

 665 + date_str = row.get( "Date" , "" )

 666 + act_type = row.get( "Type" , "" )

 667 + if account not in by_account:

 668 + by_account[account] = { "dates" : [], "types" : [], "count" : 0 }

 669 + by_account[account][ "dates" ].append(date_str)

 670 + by_account[account][ "types" ].append(act_type)

 671 + by_account[account][ "count" ] += 1

 672 +

 673 + # Build exclusion set from active customers

 674 + try :

 675 + import tools.csv_importer as csv_importer

 676 + active_accounts = csv_importer.get_active_accounts()

 677 + active_keys = set ()

 678 + for acc in active_accounts:

 679 + name = (acc.get( "Active Account Name" , "" ) or acc.get( "Display Name" , "" )).strip()

 680 + if name:

 681 + active_keys.add(name.lower())

 682 + active_keys.add(csv_importer.normalize_name(name))

 683 + except Exception:

 684 + active_keys = set ()

 685 +

 686 + today = dt.now().date()

 687 + dormant = []

 688 +

 689 + for account, data in by_account.items():

 690 + # Skip active customers

 691 + if account.lower() in active_keys or csv_importer.normalize_name(account) in active_keys:

 692 + continue

 693 +

 694 + # Find most recent activity date

 695 + latest_date = None

 696 + latest_type = ""

 697 + for i, d in enumerate (data[ "dates" ]):

 698 + try :

 699 + parsed = dt.strptime(d, "%Y-%m-%d" ).date()

 700 + if latest_date is None or parsed > latest_date:

 701 + latest_date = parsed

 702 + latest_type = data[ "types" ][i]

 703 + except (Value Error, Type Error):

 704 + continue

 705 +

 706 + if latest_date is None :

 707 + continue

 708 +

 709 + days_dormant = (today - latest_date).days

 710 + if days_dormant >= days:

 711 + dormant.append({

 712 + "account" : account,

 713 + "last_date" : latest_date.strftime( "%Y-%m-%d" ),

 714 + "last_type" : latest_type,

 715 + "days_dormant" : days_dormant,

 716 + "activity_count" : data[ "count" ],

 717 + })

 718 +

 719 + dormant.sort(key= lambda x: x[ "days_dormant" ], reverse= True )

 720 + return dormant

 721 +

 722 + except Exception as e:

 723 + logger.error( f"get_dormant_accounts error: {e} " , exc_info= True )

 724 + return []

 725 +

 726 +

 727 + def format_dormant_for_telegram ( dormant: list , limit: int = 20 ) -> str :

 728 + """Format dormant accounts for Telegram display."""

 729 + if not dormant:

 730 + return "No dormant accounts found."

 731 +

 732 + lines = [ f"💤 *Dormant Accounts* ( { len (dormant)} total)\n" ]

 733 + for i, d in enumerate (dormant[:limit], 1 ):

 734 + lines.append(

 735 + f" {i} . * {d[ 'account' ]} *\n"

 736 + f" Last: {d[ 'last_date' ]} ( {d[ 'days_dormant' ]} d ago) — {d[ 'last_type' ]} "

 737 + f" | {d[ 'activity_count' ]} total activities"

 738 + )

 739 + if len (dormant) > limit:

 740 + lines.append( f"\n ... and { len (dormant) - limit} more" )

 741 + return "\n" .join(lines)

 ⏺ Block 8: Pilot Sequence Template (#15)

 ⏺ Update ( prompts/sequence_templates.md )

 ⎿   Added 27 lines

 479 ---

 481 + ---

 482 +

 483 + ## ARCHETYPE: Pilot Offer

 484 +

 485 + **Target:** Any district/school contact considering CodeCombat — warm leads, signal-driven prospects,

 486 + conference contacts, webinar attendees, or districts in evaluation mode.

 487 +

 488 + **Core angle:** Zero-risk pilot — give CodeCombat a real test drive with your students and teachers

 489 + before committing. No budget needed, no contracts, no obligations.

 490 +

 491 + **Sequence structure (4 steps, 12 days):**

 492 +

 493 + | Step | Day | Label | Goal |

 494 + |------|-----|-------|------|

 495 + | 1 | 0 | Pilot invite | Offer the pilot: X licenses, Y weeks, zero commitment. Lead with what they get, not what it costs. Under 80 words. CTA: "Would a free pilot be useful for your team?" |

 496 + | 2 | 3 | Social proof | Pattern framing: "Most educators I work with find that students are writing real Python code by the end of week one." Reference engagement data (99% teacher satisfactio

 + n, 850K+ lines of code per student). |

 497 + | 3 | 7 | Implementation ease | Zero prep required — no coding background needed for teachers (2/3 of successful teachers started with zero experience). Turn-key slides, embedded guidance, auto

 + -grading. Address the "sounds like a lot of work" objection. |

 498 + | 4 | 12 | Deadline + booking | Friendly urgency: pilot spots are limited for this term. Direct Zoom booking link. Short — under 50 words. |

 499 +

 500 + **Tone:** Peer-to-peer, not salesy. Steven is offering something genuinely useful, not asking for a favor.

 501 + Frame as "let your students try it" not "let me demo it for you." The pilot sells itself through student engagement.

 502 +

 503 + **Pilot details:** Steven provides specific pilot terms (license count, duration, start date) when building.

 504 + If not specified, default to "30 licenses for 6 weeks."

 505 +

 506 + ---

 507 +

 508 # A/B TESTING GUIDANCE

 510 When building a sequence, Scout can generate A/B variants. Best candidates:

 WHAT COULD COME NEXT (new territory)

 Since 22/24 strategies are built and the signal system has 19 sources with 31 commands, Scout's core prospecting infrastructure is essentially complete. Future work shifts from building features to operating

 and optimizing :

 1. Live-test everything — deploy, run each scanner, validate output quality, tune filters

 2. Act on signals — the system is generating signals. Time to use /signal_act to research the top ones, build sequences, and start outreach campaigns

 3. Sequence building campaigns — use the pilot template, build AI Algebra sequences, build Cybersecurity pre-launch sequences using the targets from the scanners

 4. Monthly improvement cadence — check for new RSS feeds, tune BoardDocs districts list, adjust signal scoring weights based on what converts

 5. Firecrawl (when budget allows) — unlocks 10-20x more verified contacts per district research run

 6. Usage-based prospecting (#2) — when CodeCombat provides the data

 7. Dashboard / reporting — aggregate signal → prospect → research → sequence → reply pipeline metrics

 The machine is built. Now it's about running it.

 - `/signal_leadership` command. BoardDocs keyword expansion for superintendent search/resignation/appointment agenda items. Display-layer risk flagging: active customer leadership changes show " ⚠️

 - results on first run (expected — RFPs are procurement postings, not news). Weekly Monday 8:15 AM CST. `/signal_rfp` command.

 - ly 1st Monday 8:30 AM CST. `/signal_legislation` command. Urgency: enacted/signed = urgent, passed committee = time_sensitive.

 - All Districts (gray clustered, 7978). Clickable popups. Layer toggles. Sent as Telegram file attachment (10.3 MB). `/territory_map [state]` command. `folium>=0.17.0` added.

 15 - - **BoardDocs noise filtering:** `_BOARD_FALSE_POSITIVE` regex rejects tech matches near wheelchair, food service, janitorial, expo, fair, family night, athletic, playground. Checks 50 chars bef

 - otpedia (HTTP scraping), Job postings (JobSpy), Leadership scan (Serper + Claude), RFP scan (Serper + Claude), Legislative scan (Serper + Claude), Grant scan (Serper + Claude), Budget scan (Ser
 - per + Claude), Algebra scan (Serper + Claude), Cybersecurity scan (Serper + Claude). Signals Database tab (18 columns including Pipeline Link). 22 Telegram commands, all direct-dispatch. Daily

 nce (strong/moderate/weak/none). A $6.2B bond can be WEAK if it's all devices. A no-dollar STEAM coordinator hire can be STRONG. Auto-runs on Tier 1 during daily scans; manual via `/signal_enri

 ch N`.

 ⎿   Added 106 lines

 633 lines.append( f" {label} : {d[ 'actual' ]} / {d[ 'target' ]} ( {d[ 'pct' ]} %)" )

 635 return "\n" .join(lines)

 636 +

 637 +

 638 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 639 + # DORMANT LEAD DETECTION (#12)

 640 + # ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 641 +

 642 + def get_dormant_accounts ( days: int = 90 ) -> list [ dict ]:

 643 + """

 644 + Find accounts with Scout-tracked activity that went silent.

 645 + Only returns accounts that HAVE had activity (at least 1 logged event)

 646 + but whose most recent activity is >= `days` ago.

 647 + Excludes current active customers.

 648 +

 649 + Returns list of dicts sorted by days_dormant descending:

 650 + [{account, last_date, last_type, days_dormant, activity_count}]

 651 + """

 652 + from datetime import datetime as dt

 653 +

 654 + try :

 655 + all_rows = _load_activity_rows() # No date filter — all history

 656 + if not all_rows:

 657 + return []

 658 +

 659 + # Group by account

 660 + by_account = {}

 661 + for row in all_rows:

 662 + account = (row.get( "District/Account" ) or "" ).strip()

 663 + if not account:

 664 + continue

 665 + date_str = row.get( "Date" , "" )

 666 + act_type = row.get( "Type" , "" )

 667 + if account not in by_account:

 668 + by_account[account] = { "dates" : [], "types" : [], "count" : 0 }

 669 + by_account[account][ "dates" ].append(date_str)

 670 + by_account[account][ "types" ].append(act_type)

 671 + by_account[account][ "count" ] += 1

 672 +

 673 + # Build exclusion set from active customers

 674 + try :

 675 + import tools.csv_importer as csv_importer

 676 + active_accounts = csv_importer.get_active_accounts()

 677 + active_keys = set ()

 678 + for acc in active_accounts:

 679 + name = (acc.get( "Active Account Name" , "" ) or acc.get( "Display Name" , "" )).strip()

 680 + if name:

 681 + active_keys.add(name.lower())

 682 + active_keys.add(csv_importer.normalize_name(name))

 683 + except Exception:

 684 + active_keys = set ()

 685 +

 686 + today = dt.now().date()

 687 + dormant = []

 688 +

 689 + for account, data in by_account.items():

 690 + # Skip active customers

 691 + if account.lower() in active_keys or csv_importer.normalize_name(account) in active_keys:

 692 + continue

 693 +

 694 + # Find most recent activity date

 695 + latest_date = None

 696 + latest_type = ""

 697 + for i, d in enumerate (data[ "dates" ]):

 698 + try :

 699 + parsed = dt.strptime(d, "%Y-%m-%d" ).date()

 700 + if latest_date is None or parsed > latest_date:

 701 + latest_date = parsed

 702 + latest_type = data[ "types" ][i]

 703 + except (Value Error, Type Error):

 704 + continue

 705 +

 706 + if latest_date is None :

 707 + continue

 708 +

 709 + days_dormant = (today - latest_date).days

 710 + if days_dormant >= days:

 711 + dormant.append({

 712 + "account" : account,

 713 + "last_date" : latest_date.strftime( "%Y-%m-%d" ),

 714 + "last_type" : latest_type,

 715 + "days_dormant" : days_dormant,

 716 + "activity_count" : data[ "count" ],

 717 + })

 718 +

 719 + dormant.sort(key= lambda x: x[ "days_dormant" ], reverse= True )

 720 + return dormant

 721 +

 722 + except Exception as e:

 723 + logger.error( f"get_dormant_accounts error: {e} " , exc_info= True )

 724 + return []

 725 +

 726 +

 727 + def format_dormant_for_telegram ( dormant: list , limit: int = 20 ) -> str :

 728 + """Format dormant accounts for Telegram display."""

 729 + if not dormant:

 730 + return "No dormant accounts found."

 731 +

 732 + lines = [ f"💤 *Dormant Accounts* ( { len (dormant)} total)\n" ]

 733 + for i, d in enumerate (dormant[:limit], 1 ):

 734 + lines.append(

 735 + f" {i} . * {d[ 'account' ]} *\n"

 736 + f" Last: {d[ 'last_date' ]} ( {d[ 'days_dormant' ]} d ago) — {d[ 'last_type' ]} "

 737 + f" | {d[ 'activity_count' ]} total activities"

 738 + )

 739 + if len (dormant) > limit:

 740 + lines.append( f"\n ... and { len (dormant) - limit} more" )

 741 + return "\n" .join(lines)

 ⏺ Block 8: Pilot Sequence Template (#15)

 Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⏺ Update ( prompts/sequence_templates.md )

 ⎿   Added 27 lines

 479 ---

 481 + ---

 482 +

 483 + ## ARCHETYPE: Pilot Offer

 484 +

 485 + **Target:** Any district/school contact considering CodeCombat — warm leads, signal-driven prospects,

 486 + conference contacts, webinar attendees, or districts in evaluation mode.

 487 +

 488 + **Core angle:** Zero-risk pilot — give CodeCombat a real test drive with your students and teachers

 489 + before committing. No budget needed, no contracts, no obligations.

 490 +

 491 + **Sequence structure (4 steps, 12 days):**

 492 +

 493 + | Step | Day | Label | Goal |

 494 + |------|-----|-------|------|

 495 + | 1 | 0 | Pilot invite | Offer the pilot: X licenses, Y weeks, zero commitment. Lead with what they get, not what it costs. Under 80 words. CTA: "Would a free pilot be useful for your team?" |

 496 + | 2 | 3 | Social proof | Pattern framing: "Most educators I work with find that students are writing real Python code by the end of week one." Reference engagement data (99% teacher satisfactio

 + n, 850K+ lines of code per student). |

 497 + | 3 | 7 | Implementation ease | Zero prep required — no coding background needed for teachers (2/3 of successful teachers started with zero experience). Turn-key slides, embedded guidance, auto

 + -grading. Address the "sounds like a lot of work" objection. |

 498 + | 4 | 12 | Deadline + booking | Friendly urgency: pilot spots are limited for this term. Direct Zoom booking link. Short — under 50 words. |

 499 +

 500 + **Tone:** Peer-to-peer, not salesy. Steven is offering something genuinely useful, not asking for a favor.

 501 + Frame as "let your students try it" not "let me demo it for you." The pilot sells itself through student engagement.

 502 +

 503 + **Pilot details:** Steven provides specific pilot terms (license count, duration, start date) when building.

 504 + If not specified, default to "30 licenses for 6 weeks."

 505 +

 506 + ---

 507 +

 508 # A/B TESTING GUIDANCE

 510 When building a sequence, Scout can generate A/B variants. Best candidates:

 WHAT COULD COME NEXT (new territory)

 Since 22/24 strategies are built and the signal system has 19 sources with 31 commands, Scout's core prospecting infrastructure is essentially complete. Future work shifts from building features to operating

 and optimizing :

 1. Live-test everything — deploy, run each scanner, validate output quality, tune filters

 2. Act on signals — the system is generating signals. Time to use /signal_act to research the top ones, build sequences, and start outreach campaigns

 3. Sequence building campaigns — use the pilot template, build AI Algebra sequences, build Cybersecurity pre-launch sequences using the targets from the scanners

 4. Monthly improvement cadence — check for new RSS feeds, tune BoardDocs districts list, adjust signal scoring weights based on what converts

 5. Firecrawl (when budget allows) — unlocks 10-20x more verified contacts per district research run

 6. Usage-based prospecting (#2) — when CodeCombat provides the data

 7. Dashboard / reporting — aggregate signal → prospect → research → sequence → reply pipeline metrics

 The machine is built. Now it's about running it.

 21 ### What was done (Session 46)

 11 - - **Leadership change monitoring:** `scan_leadership_changes()` — Serper + Claude Haiku across 12 territory states. 8 superintendent changes found on first run (OH, MI). Weekly Monday 8 AM CST.

 - `/signal_leadership` command. BoardDocs keyword expansion for superintendent search/resignation/appointment agenda items. Display-layer risk flagging: active customer leadership changes show " ⚠️

 - ACCOUNT RISK" prefix.

 12 - - **RFP monitoring:** `scan_rfp_opportunities()` — Serper + Claude Haiku with aggressive CodeCombat-relevance filtering (CS/STEM/CTE curriculum only, hard-excludes construction/food/devices). 0

 - results on first run (expected — RFPs are procurement postings, not news). Weekly Monday 8:15 AM CST. `/signal_rfp` command.

 13 - - **Legislative signal scanner:** `scan_legislative_signals()` — state CS/STEM/CTE education mandates and policy changes. 2 signals found: TX HB 1481 tech mandate, IL CS education mandate. Month

 - ly 1st Monday 8:30 AM CST. `/signal_legislation` command. Urgency: enacted/signed = urgent, passed committee = time_sensitive.

 14 - - **Territory map visualization:** `tools/territory_map.py` — interactive Folium HTML with 5 layers: Active Accounts (green, 18), Pipeline (orange, 12), Prospects (blue, 108), ESAs (purple, 0),

 - All Districts (gray clustered, 7978). Clickable popups. Layer toggles. Sent as Telegram file attachment (10.3 MB). `/territory_map [state]` command. `folium>=0.17.0` added.

 15 - - **BoardDocs noise filtering:** `_BOARD_FALSE_POSITIVE` regex rejects tech matches near wheelchair, food service, janitorial, expo, fair, family night, athletic, playground. Checks 50 chars bef

 - ore + 150 chars after each keyword match.

 212 - **`signal_processor.py` is a flat module imported at top of main.py.** 14 signal sources: Google Alerts (regex), Burbio (Claude), DOE (Claude), RSS (feedparser), BoardDocs (HTTP scraping), Ball

 - otpedia (HTTP scraping), Job postings (JobSpy), Leadership scan (Serper + Claude), RFP scan (Serper + Claude), Legislative scan (Serper + Claude), Grant scan (Serper + Claude), Budget scan (Ser

 - per + Claude), Algebra scan (Serper + Claude), Cybersecurity scan (Serper + Claude). Signals Database tab (18 columns including Pipeline Link). 22 Telegram commands, all direct-dispatch. Daily

 - scan at 7:45 AM CST. Weekly Monday: leadership 8 AM + RFP 8:15 AM. Monthly 1st Monday: legislation 8:30 AM + grants 8:45 AM + budget 9:00 AM. Algebra/cybersecurity scans are on -demand only (`/s

 - ignal_algebra` , `/signal_cyber`). All ~$0 . 03/scan each. BoardDocs/Ballotpedia/RSS wrapped in try/except (non-fatal). `_last_signal_batch` is in-memory — run `/signals` before `/signal_act` or `

 - /signal_info` in a new session.

 212 + **`signal_processor.py` is a flat module imported at top of main.py.** 19 signal sources: Google Alerts (regex), Burbio (Claude), DOE (Claude), RSS ( 4 feeds, feedparser), BoardDocs (HTTP scrapi

 + ng), Ballotpedia (HTTP scraping), Job postings (JobSpy), Leadership scan (Serper + Claude), RFP scan (Serper + Claude), Legislative scan (Serper + Claude), Grant scan (Serper + Claude), Budget

 + scan (Serper + Claude), Algebra scan (Serper + Claude), Cybersecurity scan (Serper + Claude) , Role/title scan (Serper + Claude, ~$2 . 50), CSTA scan (Serper + Claude, ~$1.20). Signals Database ta

 + b (18 columns including Pipeline Link). 31 Telegram commands, all direct-dispatch. Daily scan at 7:45 AM CST. Weekly Monday: leadership 8 AM + RFP 8:15 AM. Monthly 1st Monday: legislation 8:30

 + AM + grants 8:45 AM + budget 9:00 AM. On -demand : algebra, cyber , roles, CSTA . BoardDocs/Ballotpedia/RSS wrapped in try/except (non-fatal). `_last_signal_batch` is in-memory — run `/signals` bef

 + ore `/signal_act` or `/signal_info` in a new session.

 214 **Signal enrichment must run before acting on signals.** Never queue a district based on a headline alone. `enrich_signal()` does Serper web search + Claude Haiku analysis for CodeCombat releva

 nce (strong/moderate/weak/none). A $6.2B bond can be WEAK if it's all devices. A no-dollar STEAM coordinator hire can be STRONG. Auto-runs on Tier 1 during daily scans; manual via `/signal_enri

 ch N`.

 ⏺ Block 8: Pilot Sequence Template (#15)

 Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⏺ Update ( prompts/sequence_templates.md )

 ⎿   Added 27 lines

 479 ---

 481 + ---

 482 +

 483 + ## ARCHETYPE: Pilot Offer

 484 +

 485 + **Target:** Any district/school contact considering CodeCombat — warm leads, signal-driven prospects,

 486 + conference contacts, webinar attendees, or districts in evaluation mode.

 487 +

 488 + **Core angle:** Zero-risk pilot — give CodeCombat a real test drive with your students and teachers

 489 + before committing. No budget needed, no contracts, no obligations.

 490 +

 491 + **Sequence structure (4 steps, 12 days):**

 492 +

 493 + | Step | Day | Label | Goal |

 494 + |------|-----|-------|------|

 495 + | 1 | 0 | Pilot invite | Offer the pilot: X licenses, Y weeks, zero commitment. Lead with what they get, not what it costs. Under 80 words. CTA: "Would a free pilot be useful for your team?" |

 496 + | 2 | 3 | Social proof | Pattern framing: "Most educators I work with find that students are writing real Python code by the end of week one." Reference engagement data (99% teacher satisfactio

 + n, 850K+ lines of code per student). |

 497 + | 3 | 7 | Implementation ease | Zero prep required — no coding background needed for teachers (2/3 of successful teachers started with zero experience). Turn-key slides, embedded guidance, auto

 + -grading. Address the "sounds like a lot of work" objection. |

 498 + | 4 | 12 | Deadline + booking | Friendly urgency: pilot spots are limited for this term. Direct Zoom booking link. Short — under 50 words. |

 499 +

 500 + **Tone:** Peer-to-peer, not salesy. Steven is offering something genuinely useful, not asking for a favor.

 501 + Frame as "let your students try it" not "let me demo it for you." The pilot sells itself through student engagement.

 502 +

 503 + **Pilot details:** Steven provides specific pilot terms (license count, duration, start date) when building.

 504 + If not specified, default to "30 licenses for 6 weeks."

 505 +

 506 + ---

 507 +

 508 # A/B TESTING GUIDANCE

 510 When building a sequence, Scout can generate A/B variants. Best candidates:

 WHAT COULD COME NEXT (new territory)

 Since 22/24 strategies are built and the signal system has 19 sources with 31 commands, Scout's core prospecting infrastructure is essentially complete. Future work shifts from building features to operating

 and optimizing :

 1. Live-test everything — deploy, run each scanner, validate output quality, tune filters

 2. Act on signals — the system is generating signals. Time to use /signal_act to research the top ones, build sequences, and start outreach campaigns

 3. Sequence building campaigns — use the pilot template, build AI Algebra sequences, build Cybersecurity pre-launch sequences using the targets from the scanners

 4. Monthly improvement cadence — check for new RSS feeds, tune BoardDocs districts list, adjust signal scoring weights based on what converts

 5. Firecrawl (when budget allows) — unlocks 10-20x more verified contacts per district research run

 6. Usage-based prospecting (#2) — when CodeCombat provides the data

 7. Dashboard / reporting — aggregate signal → prospect → research → sequence → reply pipeline metrics

 The machine is built. Now it's about running it.

 8 - **Session 46: 5 features shipped. Leadership change monitoring (8 superintendent turnovers found). RFP monitoring (CodeCombat-relevant filtering). Legislative signal scanner (2 CS mandates: TX H

 - B 1481, IL CS mandate). Territory map visualization (interactive Folium HTML, 10.3MB, sent via Telegram). BoardDocs noise filtering (false positive rejection). Next: verify Google Alert parser ~

 8 + **Session 47: 15 features shipped. 22 of 24 prospecting strategies now built. Signal system: 19 sources, 31 commands. New scanners: grant (#20), budget (#21), AI Algebra (#23), Cybersecurity (#2

 + 4), role/title (#7), CSTA (#8). Prospecting: lookalike (#22), sequence re-engagement (#11). Territory map: enriched popups + signal heat overlay. Dormant detection (#12), webinar tags (#14), pil

 + ot template (#15), fuzzy matching, District Administration RSS. Next: live-test on Railway, check Tulsa bond results, verify Google Alert parser ~April 9, shift to operating mode.**

 10 + ### What was done (Session 47)

 11 + - **Territory map enriched popups + signal heat overlay.** Active Accounts show licenses, revenue, enrollment. Pipeline shows opp name, close date. HeatMap layer shows signal density (togglable)

 + .

 12 + - **6 new signal scanners:** grant (#20 `/signal_grants`), budget (#21 `/signal_budget`), AI Algebra (#23 `/signal_algebra`), Cybersecurity (#24 `/signal_cyber`), role/title (#7 `/signal_roles`,

 + ~$2.50/scan), CSTA (#8 `/signal_csta`, ~$1.20/scan).

 13 + - **Cohort/lookalike prospecting (#22):** `/prospect_lookalike` — NCES districts demographically similar to best customers. $0.

 14 + - **Sequence re-engagement (#11):** `/prospect_reengagement` — scans ALL Outreach sequences for finished/no-reply prospects. Segments: engaged/lurker/ghost.

 15 + - **Dormant lead detection (#12):** `/dormant [days]` — accounts with past activity that went silent. Requires >= 1 prior activity.

 16 + - **Webinar campaign tags (#14):** CSV routing recognizes "webinar"/"attendee"/"registrant".

 17 + - **Pilot sequence template (#15):** 4-step, 12-day pilot offer archetype.

 18 + - **Fuzzy name matching:** Token subset + Jaccard for territory cross-check and map coords.

 19 + - **District Administration RSS:** Only verified working feed of 6 tested. Ed Week/THE Journal/Gov Tech broken XML.

 20 +

 21 ### What was done (Session 46)

 11 - - **Leadership change monitoring:** `scan_leadership_changes()` — Serper + Claude Haiku across 12 territory states. 8 superintendent changes found on first run (OH, MI). Weekly Monday 8 AM CST.

 - `/signal_leadership` command. BoardDocs keyword expansion for superintendent search/resignation/appointment agenda items. Display-layer risk flagging: active customer leadership changes show " ⚠️

 - ACCOUNT RISK" prefix.

 12 - - **RFP monitoring:** `scan_rfp_opportunities()` — Serper + Claude Haiku with aggressive CodeCombat-relevance filtering (CS/STEM/CTE curriculum only, hard-excludes construction/food/devices). 0

 - results on first run (expected — RFPs are procurement postings, not news). Weekly Monday 8:15 AM CST. `/signal_rfp` command.

 13 - - **Legislative signal scanner:** `scan_legislative_signals()` — state CS/STEM/CTE education mandates and policy changes. 2 signals found: TX HB 1481 tech mandate, IL CS education mandate. Month

 - ly 1st Monday 8:30 AM CST. `/signal_legislation` command. Urgency: enacted/signed = urgent, passed committee = time_sensitive.

 14 - - **Territory map visualization:** `tools/territory_map.py` — interactive Folium HTML with 5 layers: Active Accounts (green, 18), Pipeline (orange, 12), Prospects (blue, 108), ESAs (purple, 0),

 - All Districts (gray clustered, 7978). Clickable popups. Layer toggles. Sent as Telegram file attachment (10.3 MB). `/territory_map [state]` command. `folium>=0.17.0` added.

 15 - - **BoardDocs noise filtering:** `_BOARD_FALSE_POSITIVE` regex rejects tech matches near wheelchair, food service, janitorial, expo, fair, family night, athletic, playground. Checks 50 chars bef

 - ore + 150 chars after each keyword match.

 16 - - **Permissions fix:** Global `Bash(*)` in `~/.claude/settings.json` and project `.claude/settings.local.json`. Deny list still blocks rm -rf, dd, mkfs. No more babysitting permission prompts.

 22 + - Leadership change monitoring, RFP monitoring, legislative scanner, territory map visualization, BoardDocs noise filtering.

 24 ### What was done (Session 45)

 25 - Signal system expanded: RSS (3 feeds), BoardDocs (25 districts), Ballotpedia bonds, signal-to-deal attribution. 3 Outreach sequences. Sequence copy rules + send schedules.

 41 ### Current status

 42 - All prior phases + enhancements: ✅

 42 - - Signal Intelligence System: ✅ (Session 44-47) — 14 sources (Google Alerts, Burbio, DOE, RSS, BoardDocs, Ballotpedia, Job postings, Leadership scan, RFP scan, Legislative scan, Grant scan, Bud

 - get scan, Algebra scan, Cybersecurity scan). 22 Telegram commands. Daily 7:45 AM scan + weekly Monday (leadership 8 AM, RFP 8:15 AM) + monthly 1st Monday (legislation 8:30 AM, grants 8:45 AM, bu

 - dget 9:00 AM). Signal-to-deal attribution wired.

 43 + - Signal Intelligence System: ✅ (Session 44-47) — 19 sources. 31 Telegram commands. Daily 7:45 AM + weekly Monday (leadership/RFP) + monthly 1st Monday (legislation/grants/budget). On-demand: r

 + oles, CSTA, algebra, cyber. Signal-to-deal attribution wired.

 44 + - Prospecting strategies: ✅ 22 of 24 built. Only #2 (usage-based, blocked on CodeCombat data) remains.

 45 - Outreach sequences: ✅ — IDs 1995-2001 (C4 x4, License Request, Webinar x2). 3 send schedules.

 46 - Sequence copy rules: ✅ — Comprehensive rules in memory. Seasonal calendar. Send schedules.

 ⎿   Added 1 line, removed 1 line

 210 **ESA_PATTERNS in target_roles.py has 78 entity name variations.** From Steven's ROLES and KEYWORDS doc. Covers ESC, BOCES, IU, COE, ROE, ISC, ESU, AEA, CESA, SELPA, JPA, SSA, and dozens more.

 212 - **`signal_processor.py` is a flat module imported at top of main.py.** 14 signal sources: Google Alerts (regex), Burbio (Claude), DOE (Claude), RSS (feedparser), BoardDocs (HTTP scraping), Ball

 - otpedia (HTTP scraping), Job postings (JobSpy), Leadership scan (Serper + Claude), RFP scan (Serper + Claude), Legislative scan (Serper + Claude), Grant scan (Serper + Claude), Budget scan (Ser

 - per + Claude), Algebra scan (Serper + Claude), Cybersecurity scan (Serper + Claude). Signals Database tab (18 columns including Pipeline Link). 22 Telegram commands, all direct-dispatch. Daily

 - scan at 7:45 AM CST. Weekly Monday: leadership 8 AM + RFP 8:15 AM. Monthly 1st Monday: legislation 8:30 AM + grants 8:45 AM + budget 9:00 AM. Algebra/cybersecurity scans are on -demand only (`/s

 - ignal_algebra` , `/signal_cyber`). All ~$0 . 03/scan each. BoardDocs/Ballotpedia/RSS wrapped in try/except (non-fatal). `_last_signal_batch` is in-memory — run `/signals` before `/signal_act` or `

 - /signal_info` in a new session.

 212 + **`signal_processor.py` is a flat module imported at top of main.py.** 19 signal sources: Google Alerts (regex), Burbio (Claude), DOE (Claude), RSS ( 4 feeds, feedparser), BoardDocs (HTTP scrapi

 + ng), Ballotpedia (HTTP scraping), Job postings (JobSpy), Leadership scan (Serper + Claude), RFP scan (Serper + Claude), Legislative scan (Serper + Claude), Grant scan (Serper + Claude), Budget

 + scan (Serper + Claude), Algebra scan (Serper + Claude), Cybersecurity scan (Serper + Claude) , Role/title scan (Serper + Claude, ~$2 . 50), CSTA scan (Serper + Claude, ~$1.20). Signals Database ta

 + b (18 columns including Pipeline Link). 31 Telegram commands, all direct-dispatch. Daily scan at 7:45 AM CST. Weekly Monday: leadership 8 AM + RFP 8:15 AM. Monthly 1st Monday: legislation 8:30

 + AM + grants 8:45 AM + budget 9:00 AM. On -demand : algebra, cyber , roles, CSTA . BoardDocs/Ballotpedia/RSS wrapped in try/except (non-fatal). `_last_signal_batch` is in-memory — run `/signals` bef

 + ore `/signal_act` or `/signal_info` in a new session.

 214 **Signal enrichment must run before acting on signals.** Never queue a district based on a headline alone. `enrich_signal()` does Serper web search + Claude Haiku analysis for CodeCombat releva

 nce (strong/moderate/weak/none). A $6.2B bond can be WEAK if it's all devices. A no-dollar STEAM coordinator hire can be STRONG. Auto-runs on Tier 1 during daily scans; manual via `/signal_enri

 ch N`.

 ⏺ Update ( prompts/sequence_templates.md )

 ⎿   Added 27 lines

 479 ---

 481 + ---

 482 +

 483 + ## ARCHETYPE: Pilot Offer

 484 +

 485 + **Target:** Any district/school contact considering CodeCombat — warm leads, signal-driven prospects,

 486 + conference contacts, webinar attendees, or districts in evaluation mode.

 487 +

 488 + **Core angle:** Zero-risk pilot — give CodeCombat a real test drive with your students and teachers

 489 + before committing. No budget needed, no contracts, no obligations.

 490 +

 491 + **Sequence structure (4 steps, 12 days):**

 492 +

 493 + | Step | Day | Label | Goal |

 494 + |------|-----|-------|------|

 495 + | 1 | 0 | Pilot invite | Offer the pilot: X licenses, Y weeks, zero commitment. Lead with what they get, not what it costs. Under 80 words. CTA: "Would a free pilot be useful for your team?" |

 496 + | 2 | 3 | Social proof | Pattern framing: "Most educators I work with find that students are writing real Python code by the end of week one." Reference engagement data (99% teacher satisfactio

 + n, 850K+ lines of code per student). |

 497 + | 3 | 7 | Implementation ease | Zero prep required — no coding background needed for teachers (2/3 of successful teachers started with zero experience). Turn-key slides, embedded guidance, auto

 + -grading. Address the "sounds like a lot of work" objection. |

 498 + | 4 | 12 | Deadline + booking | Friendly urgency: pilot spots are limited for this term. Direct Zoom booking link. Short — under 50 words. |

 499 +

 500 + **Tone:** Peer-to-peer, not salesy. Steven is offering something genuinely useful, not asking for a favor.

 501 + Frame as "let your students try it" not "let me demo it for you." The pilot sells itself through student engagement.

 502 +

 503 + **Pilot details:** Steven provides specific pilot terms (license count, duration, start date) when building.

 504 + If not specified, default to "30 licenses for 6 weeks."

 505 +

 506 + ---

 507 +

 508 # A/B TESTING GUIDANCE

 510 When building a sequence, Scout can generate A/B variants. Best candidates:

 WHAT COULD COME NEXT (new territory)

 Since 22/24 strategies are built and the signal system has 19 sources with 31 commands, Scout's core prospecting infrastructure is essentially complete. Future work shifts from building features to operating

 and optimizing :

 1. Live-test everything — deploy, run each scanner, validate output quality, tune filters

 2. Act on signals — the system is generating signals. Time to use /signal_act to research the top ones, build sequences, and start outreach campaigns

 3. Sequence building campaigns — use the pilot template, build AI Algebra sequences, build Cybersecurity pre-launch sequences using the targets from the scanners

 4. Monthly improvement cadence — check for new RSS feeds, tune BoardDocs districts list, adjust signal scoring weights based on what converts

 5. Firecrawl (when budget allows) — unlocks 10-20x more verified contacts per district research run

 6. Usage-based prospecting (#2) — when CodeCombat provides the data

 7. Dashboard / reporting — aggregate signal → prospect → research → sequence → reply pipeline metrics

 The machine is built. Now it's about running it.

 - B 1481, IL CS mandate). Territory map visualization (interactive Folium HTML, 10.3MB, sent via Telegram). BoardDocs noise filtering (false positive rejection). Next: verify Google Alert parser ~

 8 + **Session 47: 15 features shipped. 22 of 24 prospecting strategies now built. Signal system: 19 sources, 31 commands. New scanners: grant (#20), budget (#21), AI Algebra (#23), Cybersecurity (#2

 + 4), role/title (#7), CSTA (#8). Prospecting: lookalike (#22), sequence re-engagement (#11). Territory map: enriched popups + signal heat overlay. Dormant detection (#12), webinar tags (#14), pil
