 Next: /prospect_reengagement — this is the big one. Scans all Outreach sequences for no-reply prospects.

 ❯ Ok now this one is tricky becuase each of these sequencese my have a different angle, different type of lead, different type of scenario and cant all be treated the same.

 ⏺ Good point. Let me think about this before you run it.

 The current /prospect_reengagement dumps ALL finished-no-reply prospects from ALL sequences into one bucket with strategy="sequence_reengagement". But a CUE booth visitor who ghosted needs a completely

 different re-engagement angle than a C4 cold license request who lurked, or a webinar registrant who opened but didn't reply.

 What would actually be useful here? A few options:

 1. Scan but tag by source sequence — keep the scan, but include the sequence name in the notes so you can filter/sort. You'd review the output and decide per-batch what action to take.

 2. Scan but don't auto-queue — show the results as a report (like /signals does) instead of writing to Prospecting Queue. You review, then manually /prospect_add the ones worth pursuing.

 3. Scan specific sequences only — /prospect_reengagement [sequence name or ID] so you control which sequence gets scanned and can handle each one differently.

 My take: option 3 is the right move. You pick the sequence, the results only come from that context, and you decide the re-engagement angle per sequence. The "scan everything" approach mixes too many different

 lead types.

 Want me to rework it to take a sequence ID or name filter? Or do you want to approach this differently?

 ❯ I like # 2 AND 3, but i want you to enter plan mode and really think about the best ways we coudl do this.

 ⏺ Explore (Explore reengagement + Outreach sequences)

 ⏺ User approved Claude's plan

 ⎿  Plan saved to: ~/.claude/plans/generic-knitting-pebble.md · /plan to edit

 Plan: Redesign Sequence Re-Engagement as Report-Then-Act

 Context

 Current /prospect_reengagement auto-queues ALL finished/no-reply prospects from ALL sequences into one bucket. Wrong — a CUE booth ghost needs a different angle than a license request lurker. Steven wants

 to see what's available, pick a sequence, review prospects, then decide.

 Design

 One command, two modes. No new commands — reuses existing /prospect_approve .

 Mode 1: Overview (no args)

 /prospect_reengagement

 Lists all sequences with approximate no-reply counts. Uses ONLY outreach_client.get_sequences() — a single API call that returns num_contacted and num_replied per sequence. No expensive per-prospect

 scanning.

 🔄 *Sequence Re-Engagement Overview*

 1. CUE 2026 Booth — Teachers (ID 1985)

 2. !!!2026 License Request Seq (ID 1999)

 3. Algebra Webinar — Attendees (ID 2000)

 ...12 sequences total

 Pick one to scan: /prospect_reengagement 2

 num_contacted - num_replied is approximate (includes bounced, active, etc.) but good enough for picking which sequence to drill into. Caches list in _last_reengagement_sequences .

 Mode 2: Scan One Sequence (with index + optional segment)

 /prospect_reengagement 2

 /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker

 /prospect_reengagement 2 all

 Scans ONLY the selected sequence. Default segment: engaged (highest value — they clicked or opened 3+ times). Override with lurker , ghost , or all .

 🔄 Scanning: !!!2026 License Request Seq (ID 1999)

 Found 168 finished, no-reply prospects

 🟢 Engaged: 28 | 🟡 Lurkers: 67 | ⚪ Ghosts: 73

 1. Jane Smith (jane@springfield.k12.il.us) — Springfield SD, IL

 Opens: 5, Clicks: 2

 2. Bob Johnson (bjohnson@austinisd.org) — Austin ISD, TX

 Opens: 4, Clicks: 1

 ...showing top 10 of 28

 /prospect_approve 1,3,5 to queue selected

 /prospect_approve all to queue all 28

 /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker to see lurkers instead

 Sets _last_prospect_batch to the displayed prospects. Steven uses existing /prospect_approve and /prospect_skip — no new queue command needed.

 Argument parsing logic

 - No args → overview mode

 - Number <= 100 → index into _last_reengagement_sequences (from overview)

 - Number > 100 → treat as direct Outreach sequence ID

 - Optional second word: engaged (default), lurker , ghost , all

 Implementation

 tools/district_prospector.py — replace suggest_sequence_reengagement() with:

 1. get_sequence_reengagement_overview(exclude_sequence_ids=None) → dict

 def get_sequence_reengagement_overview(exclude_sequence_ids= None ) -> dict :

 - Calls outreach_client.get_sequences() — ONE API call

 - Filters: exclude C4 IDs {1995-1998}, exclude sequences with num_contacted == 0

 - For each sequence, compute: est_no_reply = num_contacted - num_replied

 - Returns: {success, sequences: [{id, name, num_contacted, num_replied, est_no_reply, reply_rate_pct}]}

 - Sorted by est_no_reply descending (biggest opportunity first)

 - No get_sequence_states() calls — fast

 2. scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment="engaged", progress_callback=None) → dict

 def scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment= "engaged" , progress_callback= None ) -> dict :

 - Calls outreach_client.get_sequence_states(sequence_id, include_prospect=True) — one sequence only

 - Filters to state == "finished" AND reply_count == 0

 - Segments each prospect: engaged (opens>=3 or clicks>0), lurker (opens>0), ghost (opens==0)

 - Applies exclusion filters: active customers, existing prospects, pipeline, territory check

 - Returns ALL candidates with segment labels, BUT only populates display list with requested segment

 - Returns: {success, sequence_name, total_no_reply, segment_counts: {engaged, lurker, ghost}, candidates: [prospect dicts for requested segment]}

 - Each candidate dict has: state, company, email, first_name, last_name, name_key, segment, priority, open_count, click_count, seq_name

 3. format_reengagement_overview(result) → str

 Formats the overview for Telegram. Numbered list with reply rate.

 4. format_reengagement_scan(result, segment) → str

 Formats the scan results. Shows segment counts at top, then numbered prospect list for the selected segment. Includes "/prospect_approve" hints.

 Delete the old suggest_sequence_reengagement() function entirely — it's being replaced, not refactored. The auto-queue behavior goes away.

 agent/main.py — modify handler

 New global:

 _last_reengagement_sequences: list [ dict ] = []

 Add to the global declaration line in handle_message() .

 Replace the current /prospect_reengagement handler with:

 elif user_text.lower().startswith( "/prospect_reengagement" ) or user_text.lower() in [ "reengagement" , "re-engagement" ]:

 parts = user_text.strip().split()

 # Mode 1: no args → overview

 # Mode 2: with number → scan specific sequence

 # Optional segment: engaged (default), lurker, ghost, all

 Parse logic:

 - Extract first numeric arg (if any) → seq_selector

 - Extract segment keyword (if any) → segment (default "engaged")

 - If no numeric arg: call get_sequence_reengagement_overview() , format, cache in _last_reengagement_sequences

 - If numeric arg <= 100: look up _last_reengagement_sequences[idx-1] , get sequence_id

 - If numeric arg > 100: use directly as sequence_id

 - Call scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment) , format, set _last_prospect_batch to candidates

 No changes to /prospect_approve or /prospect_skip — they already work with _last_prospect_batch .

 Files to Modify

 - tools/district_prospector.py — delete old function, add 4 new functions

 - agent/main.py — replace handler, add global

 - tools/CLAUDE.md — update function signatures

 - CLAUDE.md — update command docs

 Edge Cases

 - Overview returns 0 sequences with prospects → "No sequences with contacted prospects found."

 - Scan returns 0 no-reply for selected segment → "No {segment} prospects in this sequence. Try /prospect_reengagement {idx} all to see all segments."

 - _last_reengagement_sequences empty when Mode 2 attempted → "Run /prospect_reengagement first to see available sequences."

 - Outreach OAuth expired → outreach_client auto-refreshes; if refresh fails, returns auth error

 - Sequence has 5000+ prospects → get_sequence_states() handles pagination (50/page × 100 pages max). Add time.sleep(0.3) between page fetches won't work since pagination is internal to

 get_sequence_states() . Rate limiting is handled by the client.

 Verification

 1. /prospect_reengagement → shows sequence list with est. no-reply counts (fast, ~2 seconds)

 2. /prospect_reengagement 2 → scans one sequence, shows engaged prospects

 3. /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker → same sequence, lurker segment

 4. /prospect_reengagement 2 all → all segments

 5. /prospect_approve 1,3 → queues selected prospects from last scan

 6. Verify: NOTHING auto-queued at any step until explicit /prospect_approve

 Changes from v1

 ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Change │ Why │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Overview uses get_sequences() only — no per-prospect │ v1 called get_sequence_states() for every sequence in overview mode. With 30 sequences, that's 30+ paginated API calls just to show counts. │

 │ scanning │ Now it's 1 call. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Killed /reengagement_queue command │ Too close to /prospect_approve . Steven already knows approve/skip. Adding a third queue mechanism is confusing. Segment filtering moves to │

 │ │ scan time instead. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Segment selection at scan time, not queue time │ "Show me engaged from CUE Booth" is one step, not two. Reduces state management and cognitive overhead. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Removed _last_reengagement_scan global │ Fewer in-memory caches = fewer "run X first" errors. _last_prospect_batch handles everything. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Delete old function entirely, don't refactor │ Old function's auto-queue behavior is fundamentally wrong. Clean break is simpler than trying to preserve parts. │

 └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 Next: /prospect_reengagement — this is the big one. Scans all Outreach sequences for no-reply prospects.

 ❯ Ok now this one is tricky becuase each of these sequencese my have a different angle, different type of lead, different type of scenario and cant all be treated the same.

 ⏺ Good point. Let me think about this before you run it.

 The current /prospect_reengagement dumps ALL finished-no-reply prospects from ALL sequences into one bucket with strategy="sequence_reengagement". But a CUE booth visitor who ghosted needs a completely

 different re-engagement angle than a C4 cold license request who lurked, or a webinar registrant who opened but didn't reply.

 What would actually be useful here? A few options:

 1. Scan but tag by source sequence — keep the scan, but include the sequence name in the notes so you can filter/sort. You'd review the output and decide per-batch what action to take.

 2. Scan but don't auto-queue — show the results as a report (like /signals does) instead of writing to Prospecting Queue. You review, then manually /prospect_add the ones worth pursuing.

 3. Scan specific sequences only — /prospect_reengagement [sequence name or ID] so you control which sequence gets scanned and can handle each one differently.

 My take: option 3 is the right move. You pick the sequence, the results only come from that context, and you decide the re-engagement angle per sequence. The "scan everything" approach mixes too many different

 lead types.

 Want me to rework it to take a sequence ID or name filter? Or do you want to approach this differently?

 ❯ I like # 2 AND 3, but i want you to enter plan mode and really think about the best ways we coudl do this.

 ⏺ Explore (Explore reengagement + Outreach sequences)

 ⏺ User approved Claude's plan

 ⎿  Plan saved to: ~/.claude/plans/generic-knitting-pebble.md · /plan to edit

 Plan: Redesign Sequence Re-Engagement as Report-Then-Act

 Context

 Current /prospect_reengagement auto-queues ALL finished/no-reply prospects from ALL sequences into one bucket. Wrong — a CUE booth ghost needs a different angle than a license request lurker. Steven wants

 to see what's available, pick a sequence, review prospects, then decide.

 Design

 One command, two modes. No new commands — reuses existing /prospect_approve .

 Mode 1: Overview (no args)

 /prospect_reengagement

 Lists all sequences with approximate no-reply counts. Uses ONLY outreach_client.get_sequences() — a single API call that returns num_contacted and num_replied per sequence. No expensive per-prospect

 scanning.

 🔄 *Sequence Re-Engagement Overview*

 1. CUE 2026 Booth — Teachers (ID 1985)

 2. !!!2026 License Request Seq (ID 1999)

 3. Algebra Webinar — Attendees (ID 2000)

 ...12 sequences total

 Pick one to scan: /prospect_reengagement 2

 num_contacted - num_replied is approximate (includes bounced, active, etc.) but good enough for picking which sequence to drill into. Caches list in _last_reengagement_sequences .

 Mode 2: Scan One Sequence (with index + optional segment)

 /prospect_reengagement 2

 /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker

 /prospect_reengagement 2 all

 Scans ONLY the selected sequence. Default segment: engaged (highest value — they clicked or opened 3+ times). Override with lurker , ghost , or all .

 🔄 Scanning: !!!2026 License Request Seq (ID 1999)

 Found 168 finished, no-reply prospects

 🟢 Engaged: 28 | 🟡 Lurkers: 67 | ⚪ Ghosts: 73

 1. Jane Smith (jane@springfield.k12.il.us) — Springfield SD, IL

 Opens: 5, Clicks: 2

 2. Bob Johnson (bjohnson@austinisd.org) — Austin ISD, TX

 Opens: 4, Clicks: 1

 ...showing top 10 of 28

 /prospect_approve 1,3,5 to queue selected

 /prospect_approve all to queue all 28

 /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker to see lurkers instead

 Sets _last_prospect_batch to the displayed prospects. Steven uses existing /prospect_approve and /prospect_skip — no new queue command needed.

 Argument parsing logic

 - No args → overview mode

 - Number <= 100 → index into _last_reengagement_sequences (from overview)

 - Number > 100 → treat as direct Outreach sequence ID

 - Optional second word: engaged (default), lurker , ghost , all

 Implementation

 tools/district_prospector.py — replace suggest_sequence_reengagement() with:

 1. get_sequence_reengagement_overview(exclude_sequence_ids=None) → dict

 def get_sequence_reengagement_overview(exclude_sequence_ids= None ) -> dict :

 - Calls outreach_client.get_sequences() — ONE API call

 - Filters: exclude C4 IDs {1995-1998}, exclude sequences with num_contacted == 0

 - For each sequence, compute: est_no_reply = num_contacted - num_replied

 - Returns: {success, sequences: [{id, name, num_contacted, num_replied, est_no_reply, reply_rate_pct}]}

 - Sorted by est_no_reply descending (biggest opportunity first)

 - No get_sequence_states() calls — fast

 2. scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment="engaged", progress_callback=None) → dict

 def scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment= "engaged" , progress_callback= None ) -> dict :

 - Calls outreach_client.get_sequence_states(sequence_id, include_prospect=True) — one sequence only

 - Filters to state == "finished" AND reply_count == 0

 - Segments each prospect: engaged (opens>=3 or clicks>0), lurker (opens>0), ghost (opens==0)

 - Applies exclusion filters: active customers, existing prospects, pipeline, territory check

 - Returns ALL candidates with segment labels, BUT only populates display list with requested segment

 - Returns: {success, sequence_name, total_no_reply, segment_counts: {engaged, lurker, ghost}, candidates: [prospect dicts for requested segment]}

 - Each candidate dict has: state, company, email, first_name, last_name, name_key, segment, priority, open_count, click_count, seq_name

 3. format_reengagement_overview(result) → str

 Formats the overview for Telegram. Numbered list with reply rate.

 4. format_reengagement_scan(result, segment) → str

 Formats the scan results. Shows segment counts at top, then numbered prospect list for the selected segment. Includes "/prospect_approve" hints.

 Delete the old suggest_sequence_reengagement() function entirely — it's being replaced, not refactored. The auto-queue behavior goes away.

 agent/main.py — modify handler

 New global:

 _last_reengagement_sequences: list [ dict ] = []

 Add to the global declaration line in handle_message() .

 Replace the current /prospect_reengagement handler with:

 elif user_text.lower().startswith( "/prospect_reengagement" ) or user_text.lower() in [ "reengagement" , "re-engagement" ]:

 parts = user_text.strip().split()

 # Mode 1: no args → overview

 # Mode 2: with number → scan specific sequence

 # Optional segment: engaged (default), lurker, ghost, all

 Parse logic:

 - Extract first numeric arg (if any) → seq_selector

 - Extract segment keyword (if any) → segment (default "engaged")

 - If no numeric arg: call get_sequence_reengagement_overview() , format, cache in _last_reengagement_sequences

 - If numeric arg <= 100: look up _last_reengagement_sequences[idx-1] , get sequence_id

 - If numeric arg > 100: use directly as sequence_id

 - Call scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment) , format, set _last_prospect_batch to candidates

 No changes to /prospect_approve or /prospect_skip — they already work with _last_prospect_batch .

 Files to Modify

 - tools/district_prospector.py — delete old function, add 4 new functions

 - agent/main.py — replace handler, add global

 - tools/CLAUDE.md — update function signatures

 - CLAUDE.md — update command docs

 Edge Cases

 - Overview returns 0 sequences with prospects → "No sequences with contacted prospects found."

 - Scan returns 0 no-reply for selected segment → "No {segment} prospects in this sequence. Try /prospect_reengagement {idx} all to see all segments."

 - _last_reengagement_sequences empty when Mode 2 attempted → "Run /prospect_reengagement first to see available sequences."

 - Outreach OAuth expired → outreach_client auto-refreshes; if refresh fails, returns auth error

 - Sequence has 5000+ prospects → get_sequence_states() handles pagination (50/page × 100 pages max). Add time.sleep(0.3) between page fetches won't work since pagination is internal to

 get_sequence_states() . Rate limiting is handled by the client.

 Verification

 1. /prospect_reengagement → shows sequence list with est. no-reply counts (fast, ~2 seconds)

 2. /prospect_reengagement 2 → scans one sequence, shows engaged prospects

 3. /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker → same sequence, lurker segment

 4. /prospect_reengagement 2 all → all segments

 5. /prospect_approve 1,3 → queues selected prospects from last scan

 6. Verify: NOTHING auto-queued at any step until explicit /prospect_approve

 Changes from v1

 ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Change │ Why │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Overview uses get_sequences() only — no per-prospect │ v1 called get_sequence_states() for every sequence in overview mode. With 30 sequences, that's 30+ paginated API calls just to show counts. │

 │ scanning │ Now it's 1 call. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Killed /reengagement_queue command │ Too close to /prospect_approve . Steven already knows approve/skip. Adding a third queue mechanism is confusing. Segment filtering moves to │

 │ │ scan time instead. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Segment selection at scan time, not queue time │ "Show me engaged from CUE Booth" is one step, not two. Reduces state management and cognitive overhead. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Removed _last_reengagement_scan global │ Fewer in-memory caches = fewer "run X first" errors. _last_prospect_batch handles everything. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Delete old function entirely, don't refactor │ Old function's auto-queue behavior is fundamentally wrong. Clean break is simpler than trying to preserve parts. │

 └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 ❯ Ok now this one is tricky becuase each of these sequencese my have a different angle, different type of lead, different type of scenario and cant all be treated the same.

 ⏺ Good point. Let me think about this before you run it.

 The current /prospect_reengagement dumps ALL finished-no-reply prospects from ALL sequences into one bucket with strategy="sequence_reengagement". But a CUE booth visitor who ghosted needs a completely

 different re-engagement angle than a C4 cold license request who lurked, or a webinar registrant who opened but didn't reply.

 What would actually be useful here? A few options:

 1. Scan but tag by source sequence — keep the scan, but include the sequence name in the notes so you can filter/sort. You'd review the output and decide per-batch what action to take.

 2. Scan but don't auto-queue — show the results as a report (like /signals does) instead of writing to Prospecting Queue. You review, then manually /prospect_add the ones worth pursuing.

 3. Scan specific sequences only — /prospect_reengagement [sequence name or ID] so you control which sequence gets scanned and can handle each one differently.

 My take: option 3 is the right move. You pick the sequence, the results only come from that context, and you decide the re-engagement angle per sequence. The "scan everything" approach mixes too many different

 lead types.

 Want me to rework it to take a sequence ID or name filter? Or do you want to approach this differently?

 ❯ I like # 2 AND 3, but i want you to enter plan mode and really think about the best ways we coudl do this.

 ⏺ Explore (Explore reengagement + Outreach sequences)

 ⏺ User approved Claude's plan

 ⎿  Plan saved to: ~/.claude/plans/generic-knitting-pebble.md · /plan to edit

 Plan: Redesign Sequence Re-Engagement as Report-Then-Act

 Context

 Current /prospect_reengagement auto-queues ALL finished/no-reply prospects from ALL sequences into one bucket. Wrong — a CUE booth ghost needs a different angle than a license request lurker. Steven wants

 to see what's available, pick a sequence, review prospects, then decide.

 Design

 One command, two modes. No new commands — reuses existing /prospect_approve .

 Mode 1: Overview (no args)

 /prospect_reengagement

 Lists all sequences with approximate no-reply counts. Uses ONLY outreach_client.get_sequences() — a single API call that returns num_contacted and num_replied per sequence. No expensive per-prospect

 scanning.

 🔄 *Sequence Re-Engagement Overview*

 1. CUE 2026 Booth — Teachers (ID 1985)

 2. !!!2026 License Request Seq (ID 1999)

 3. Algebra Webinar — Attendees (ID 2000)

 ...12 sequences total

 Pick one to scan: /prospect_reengagement 2

 num_contacted - num_replied is approximate (includes bounced, active, etc.) but good enough for picking which sequence to drill into. Caches list in _last_reengagement_sequences .

 Mode 2: Scan One Sequence (with index + optional segment)

 /prospect_reengagement 2

 /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker

 /prospect_reengagement 2 all

 Scans ONLY the selected sequence. Default segment: engaged (highest value — they clicked or opened 3+ times). Override with lurker , ghost , or all .

 🔄 Scanning: !!!2026 License Request Seq (ID 1999)

 Found 168 finished, no-reply prospects

 🟢 Engaged: 28 | 🟡 Lurkers: 67 | ⚪ Ghosts: 73

 1. Jane Smith (jane@springfield.k12.il.us) — Springfield SD, IL

 Opens: 5, Clicks: 2

 2. Bob Johnson (bjohnson@austinisd.org) — Austin ISD, TX

 Opens: 4, Clicks: 1

 ...showing top 10 of 28

 /prospect_approve 1,3,5 to queue selected

 /prospect_approve all to queue all 28

 /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker to see lurkers instead

 Sets _last_prospect_batch to the displayed prospects. Steven uses existing /prospect_approve and /prospect_skip — no new queue command needed.

 Argument parsing logic

 - No args → overview mode

 - Number <= 100 → index into _last_reengagement_sequences (from overview)

 - Number > 100 → treat as direct Outreach sequence ID

 - Optional second word: engaged (default), lurker , ghost , all

 Implementation

 tools/district_prospector.py — replace suggest_sequence_reengagement() with:

 1. get_sequence_reengagement_overview(exclude_sequence_ids=None) → dict

 def get_sequence_reengagement_overview(exclude_sequence_ids= None ) -> dict :

 - Calls outreach_client.get_sequences() — ONE API call

 - Filters: exclude C4 IDs {1995-1998}, exclude sequences with num_contacted == 0

 - For each sequence, compute: est_no_reply = num_contacted - num_replied

 - Returns: {success, sequences: [{id, name, num_contacted, num_replied, est_no_reply, reply_rate_pct}]}

 - Sorted by est_no_reply descending (biggest opportunity first)

 - No get_sequence_states() calls — fast

 2. scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment="engaged", progress_callback=None) → dict

 def scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment= "engaged" , progress_callback= None ) -> dict :

 - Calls outreach_client.get_sequence_states(sequence_id, include_prospect=True) — one sequence only

 - Filters to state == "finished" AND reply_count == 0

 - Segments each prospect: engaged (opens>=3 or clicks>0), lurker (opens>0), ghost (opens==0)

 - Applies exclusion filters: active customers, existing prospects, pipeline, territory check

 - Returns ALL candidates with segment labels, BUT only populates display list with requested segment

 - Returns: {success, sequence_name, total_no_reply, segment_counts: {engaged, lurker, ghost}, candidates: [prospect dicts for requested segment]}

 - Each candidate dict has: state, company, email, first_name, last_name, name_key, segment, priority, open_count, click_count, seq_name

 3. format_reengagement_overview(result) → str

 Formats the overview for Telegram. Numbered list with reply rate.

 4. format_reengagement_scan(result, segment) → str

 Formats the scan results. Shows segment counts at top, then numbered prospect list for the selected segment. Includes "/prospect_approve" hints.

 Delete the old suggest_sequence_reengagement() function entirely — it's being replaced, not refactored. The auto-queue behavior goes away.

 agent/main.py — modify handler

 New global:

 _last_reengagement_sequences: list [ dict ] = []

 Add to the global declaration line in handle_message() .

 Replace the current /prospect_reengagement handler with:

 elif user_text.lower().startswith( "/prospect_reengagement" ) or user_text.lower() in [ "reengagement" , "re-engagement" ]:

 parts = user_text.strip().split()

 # Mode 1: no args → overview

 # Mode 2: with number → scan specific sequence

 # Optional segment: engaged (default), lurker, ghost, all

 Parse logic:

 - Extract first numeric arg (if any) → seq_selector

 - Extract segment keyword (if any) → segment (default "engaged")

 - If no numeric arg: call get_sequence_reengagement_overview() , format, cache in _last_reengagement_sequences

 - If numeric arg <= 100: look up _last_reengagement_sequences[idx-1] , get sequence_id

 - If numeric arg > 100: use directly as sequence_id

 - Call scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment) , format, set _last_prospect_batch to candidates

 No changes to /prospect_approve or /prospect_skip — they already work with _last_prospect_batch .

 Files to Modify

 - tools/district_prospector.py — delete old function, add 4 new functions

 - agent/main.py — replace handler, add global

 - tools/CLAUDE.md — update function signatures

 - CLAUDE.md — update command docs

 Edge Cases

 - Overview returns 0 sequences with prospects → "No sequences with contacted prospects found."

 - Scan returns 0 no-reply for selected segment → "No {segment} prospects in this sequence. Try /prospect_reengagement {idx} all to see all segments."

 - _last_reengagement_sequences empty when Mode 2 attempted → "Run /prospect_reengagement first to see available sequences."

 - Outreach OAuth expired → outreach_client auto-refreshes; if refresh fails, returns auth error

 - Sequence has 5000+ prospects → get_sequence_states() handles pagination (50/page × 100 pages max). Add time.sleep(0.3) between page fetches won't work since pagination is internal to

 get_sequence_states() . Rate limiting is handled by the client.

 Verification

 1. /prospect_reengagement → shows sequence list with est. no-reply counts (fast, ~2 seconds)

 2. /prospect_reengagement 2 → scans one sequence, shows engaged prospects

 3. /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker → same sequence, lurker segment

 4. /prospect_reengagement 2 all → all segments

 5. /prospect_approve 1,3 → queues selected prospects from last scan

 6. Verify: NOTHING auto-queued at any step until explicit /prospect_approve

 Changes from v1

 ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Change │ Why │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Overview uses get_sequences() only — no per-prospect │ v1 called get_sequence_states() for every sequence in overview mode. With 30 sequences, that's 30+ paginated API calls just to show counts. │

 │ scanning │ Now it's 1 call. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Killed /reengagement_queue command │ Too close to /prospect_approve . Steven already knows approve/skip. Adding a third queue mechanism is confusing. Segment filtering moves to │

 │ │ scan time instead. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Segment selection at scan time, not queue time │ "Show me engaged from CUE Booth" is one step, not two. Reduces state management and cognitive overhead. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Removed _last_reengagement_scan global │ Fewer in-memory caches = fewer "run X first" errors. _last_prospect_batch handles everything. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Delete old function entirely, don't refactor │ Old function's auto-queue behavior is fundamentally wrong. Clean break is simpler than trying to preserve parts. │

 └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 e7cdd09..d2c1bb7 main -> main

 ⏺ Pushed. After redeploy, test the new flow:

 1. /prospect_reengagement — should show your sequences with reply rates (fast, ~2 seconds)

 2. Pick a sequence: /prospect_reengagement 2 — scans it, shows engaged prospects

 3. Try different segment: /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker

 4. Queue the ones you want: /prospect_approve 1,3,5

 ❯ [Image #9] [Image #10] [Image #11] ok i sent /prospect_reengagement here are the results. why does the reply rates matter? why is it listing all these out here in telegram?

 ⎿   [Image #9]

 ⎿   [Image #10]

 ⎿   [Image #11]

 ⏺ You're right on both counts.

 1. Reply rate doesn't matter here. The point is finding sequences with prospects worth re-engaging. The useful number is "est. no-reply" — and it's already sorted by that. Reply rate is noise.

 2. 129 sequences is absurd to list. Most have 1-5 contacts. Only the top 10-15 with meaningful volume are worth scanning.

 Quick fix — filter to sequences with 20+ no-reply, show top 15 max, drop reply rate.

 Next: /prospect_reengagement — this is the big one. Scans all Outreach sequences for no-reply prospects.

 ❯ Ok now this one is tricky becuase each of these sequencese my have a different angle, different type of lead, different type of scenario and cant all be treated the same.

 ⏺ Good point. Let me think about this before you run it.

 The current /prospect_reengagement dumps ALL finished-no-reply prospects from ALL sequences into one bucket with strategy="sequence_reengagement". But a CUE booth visitor who ghosted needs a completely

 different re-engagement angle than a C4 cold license request who lurked, or a webinar registrant who opened but didn't reply.

 What would actually be useful here? A few options:

 1. Scan but tag by source sequence — keep the scan, but include the sequence name in the notes so you can filter/sort. You'd review the output and decide per-batch what action to take.

 2. Scan but don't auto-queue — show the results as a report (like /signals does) instead of writing to Prospecting Queue. You review, then manually /prospect_add the ones worth pursuing.

 3. Scan specific sequences only — /prospect_reengagement [sequence name or ID] so you control which sequence gets scanned and can handle each one differently.

 My take: option 3 is the right move. You pick the sequence, the results only come from that context, and you decide the re-engagement angle per sequence. The "scan everything" approach mixes too many different

 lead types.

 Want me to rework it to take a sequence ID or name filter? Or do you want to approach this differently?

 ❯ I like # 2 AND 3, but i want you to enter plan mode and really think about the best ways we coudl do this.

 ⏺ Explore (Explore reengagement + Outreach sequences)

 ⏺ User approved Claude's plan

 ⎿  Plan saved to: ~/.claude/plans/generic-knitting-pebble.md · /plan to edit

 Plan: Redesign Sequence Re-Engagement as Report-Then-Act

 Context

 Current /prospect_reengagement auto-queues ALL finished/no-reply prospects from ALL sequences into one bucket. Wrong — a CUE booth ghost needs a different angle than a license request lurker. Steven wants

 to see what's available, pick a sequence, review prospects, then decide.

 Design

 One command, two modes. No new commands — reuses existing /prospect_approve .

 Mode 1: Overview (no args)

 /prospect_reengagement

 Lists all sequences with approximate no-reply counts. Uses ONLY outreach_client.get_sequences() — a single API call that returns num_contacted and num_replied per sequence. No expensive per-prospect

 scanning.

 🔄 *Sequence Re-Engagement Overview*

 1. CUE 2026 Booth — Teachers (ID 1985)

 2. !!!2026 License Request Seq (ID 1999)

 3. Algebra Webinar — Attendees (ID 2000)

 ...12 sequences total

 Pick one to scan: /prospect_reengagement 2

 num_contacted - num_replied is approximate (includes bounced, active, etc.) but good enough for picking which sequence to drill into. Caches list in _last_reengagement_sequences .

 Mode 2: Scan One Sequence (with index + optional segment)

 /prospect_reengagement 2

 /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker

 /prospect_reengagement 2 all

 Scans ONLY the selected sequence. Default segment: engaged (highest value — they clicked or opened 3+ times). Override with lurker , ghost , or all .

 🔄 Scanning: !!!2026 License Request Seq (ID 1999)

 Found 168 finished, no-reply prospects

 🟢 Engaged: 28 | 🟡 Lurkers: 67 | ⚪ Ghosts: 73

 1. Jane Smith (jane@springfield.k12.il.us) — Springfield SD, IL

 Opens: 5, Clicks: 2

 2. Bob Johnson (bjohnson@austinisd.org) — Austin ISD, TX

 Opens: 4, Clicks: 1

 ...showing top 10 of 28

 /prospect_approve 1,3,5 to queue selected

 /prospect_approve all to queue all 28

 /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker to see lurkers instead

 Sets _last_prospect_batch to the displayed prospects. Steven uses existing /prospect_approve and /prospect_skip — no new queue command needed.

 Argument parsing logic

 - No args → overview mode

 - Number <= 100 → index into _last_reengagement_sequences (from overview)

 - Number > 100 → treat as direct Outreach sequence ID

 - Optional second word: engaged (default), lurker , ghost , all

 Implementation

 tools/district_prospector.py — replace suggest_sequence_reengagement() with:

 1. get_sequence_reengagement_overview(exclude_sequence_ids=None) → dict

 def get_sequence_reengagement_overview(exclude_sequence_ids= None ) -> dict :

 - Calls outreach_client.get_sequences() — ONE API call

 - Filters: exclude C4 IDs {1995-1998}, exclude sequences with num_contacted == 0

 - For each sequence, compute: est_no_reply = num_contacted - num_replied

 - Returns: {success, sequences: [{id, name, num_contacted, num_replied, est_no_reply, reply_rate_pct}]}

 - Sorted by est_no_reply descending (biggest opportunity first)

 - No get_sequence_states() calls — fast

 2. scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment="engaged", progress_callback=None) → dict

 def scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment= "engaged" , progress_callback= None ) -> dict :

 - Calls outreach_client.get_sequence_states(sequence_id, include_prospect=True) — one sequence only

 - Filters to state == "finished" AND reply_count == 0

 - Segments each prospect: engaged (opens>=3 or clicks>0), lurker (opens>0), ghost (opens==0)

 - Applies exclusion filters: active customers, existing prospects, pipeline, territory check

 - Returns ALL candidates with segment labels, BUT only populates display list with requested segment

 - Returns: {success, sequence_name, total_no_reply, segment_counts: {engaged, lurker, ghost}, candidates: [prospect dicts for requested segment]}

 - Each candidate dict has: state, company, email, first_name, last_name, name_key, segment, priority, open_count, click_count, seq_name

 3. format_reengagement_overview(result) → str

 Formats the overview for Telegram. Numbered list with reply rate.

 4. format_reengagement_scan(result, segment) → str

 Formats the scan results. Shows segment counts at top, then numbered prospect list for the selected segment. Includes "/prospect_approve" hints.

 Delete the old suggest_sequence_reengagement() function entirely — it's being replaced, not refactored. The auto-queue behavior goes away.

 agent/main.py — modify handler

 New global:

 _last_reengagement_sequences: list [ dict ] = []

 Add to the global declaration line in handle_message() .

 Replace the current /prospect_reengagement handler with:

 elif user_text.lower().startswith( "/prospect_reengagement" ) or user_text.lower() in [ "reengagement" , "re-engagement" ]:

 parts = user_text.strip().split()

 # Mode 1: no args → overview

 # Mode 2: with number → scan specific sequence

 # Optional segment: engaged (default), lurker, ghost, all

 Parse logic:

 - Extract first numeric arg (if any) → seq_selector

 - Extract segment keyword (if any) → segment (default "engaged")

 - If no numeric arg: call get_sequence_reengagement_overview() , format, cache in _last_reengagement_sequences

 - If numeric arg <= 100: look up _last_reengagement_sequences[idx-1] , get sequence_id

 - If numeric arg > 100: use directly as sequence_id

 - Call scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment) , format, set _last_prospect_batch to candidates

 No changes to /prospect_approve or /prospect_skip — they already work with _last_prospect_batch .

 Files to Modify

 - tools/district_prospector.py — delete old function, add 4 new functions

 - agent/main.py — replace handler, add global

 - tools/CLAUDE.md — update function signatures

 - CLAUDE.md — update command docs

 Edge Cases

 - Overview returns 0 sequences with prospects → "No sequences with contacted prospects found."

 - Scan returns 0 no-reply for selected segment → "No {segment} prospects in this sequence. Try /prospect_reengagement {idx} all to see all segments."

 - _last_reengagement_sequences empty when Mode 2 attempted → "Run /prospect_reengagement first to see available sequences."

 - Outreach OAuth expired → outreach_client auto-refreshes; if refresh fails, returns auth error

 - Sequence has 5000+ prospects → get_sequence_states() handles pagination (50/page × 100 pages max). Add time.sleep(0.3) between page fetches won't work since pagination is internal to

 get_sequence_states() . Rate limiting is handled by the client.

 Verification

 1. /prospect_reengagement → shows sequence list with est. no-reply counts (fast, ~2 seconds)

 2. /prospect_reengagement 2 → scans one sequence, shows engaged prospects

 3. /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker → same sequence, lurker segment

 4. /prospect_reengagement 2 all → all segments

 5. /prospect_approve 1,3 → queues selected prospects from last scan

 6. Verify: NOTHING auto-queued at any step until explicit /prospect_approve

 Changes from v1

 ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Change │ Why │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Overview uses get_sequences() only — no per-prospect │ v1 called get_sequence_states() for every sequence in overview mode. With 30 sequences, that's 30+ paginated API calls just to show counts. │

 │ scanning │ Now it's 1 call. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Killed /reengagement_queue command │ Too close to /prospect_approve . Steven already knows approve/skip. Adding a third queue mechanism is confusing. Segment filtering moves to │

 │ │ scan time instead. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Segment selection at scan time, not queue time │ "Show me engaged from CUE Booth" is one step, not two. Reduces state management and cognitive overhead. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Removed _last_reengagement_scan global │ Fewer in-memory caches = fewer "run X first" errors. _last_prospect_batch handles everything. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Delete old function entirely, don't refactor │ Old function's auto-queue behavior is fundamentally wrong. Clean break is simpler than trying to preserve parts. │

 └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 ⏺ Plan approved. Let me execute it.

 Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⏺ Now I'll replace the old function with the new report-then-act architecture:

 ⏺ Pushed. After redeploy, test the new flow:

 1. /prospect_reengagement — should show your sequences with reply rates (fast, ~2 seconds)

 2. Pick a sequence: /prospect_reengagement 2 — scans it, shows engaged prospects

 3. Try different segment: /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker

 4. Queue the ones you want: /prospect_approve 1,3,5

 ❯ [Image #9] [Image #10] [Image #11] ok i sent /prospect_reengagement here are the results. why does the reply rates matter? why is it listing all these out here in telegram?

 ⎿   [Image #9]

 ⎿   [Image #10]

 ⎿   [Image #11]

 ⏺ You're right on both counts.

 1. Reply rate doesn't matter here. The point is finding sequences with prospects worth re-engaging. The useful number is "est. no-reply" — and it's already sorted by that. Reply rate is noise.

 2. 129 sequences is absurd to list. Most have 1-5 contacts. Only the top 10-15 with meaningful volume are worth scanning.

 Quick fix — filter to sequences with 20+ no-reply, show top 15 max, drop reply rate.

 Next: /prospect_reengagement — this is the big one. Scans all Outreach sequences for no-reply prospects.

 ❯ Ok now this one is tricky becuase each of these sequencese my have a different angle, different type of lead, different type of scenario and cant all be treated the same.

 ⏺ Good point. Let me think about this before you run it.

 The current /prospect_reengagement dumps ALL finished-no-reply prospects from ALL sequences into one bucket with strategy="sequence_reengagement". But a CUE booth visitor who ghosted needs a completely

 different re-engagement angle than a C4 cold license request who lurked, or a webinar registrant who opened but didn't reply.

 What would actually be useful here? A few options:

 1. Scan but tag by source sequence — keep the scan, but include the sequence name in the notes so you can filter/sort. You'd review the output and decide per-batch what action to take.

 2. Scan but don't auto-queue — show the results as a report (like /signals does) instead of writing to Prospecting Queue. You review, then manually /prospect_add the ones worth pursuing.

 3. Scan specific sequences only — /prospect_reengagement [sequence name or ID] so you control which sequence gets scanned and can handle each one differently.

 My take: option 3 is the right move. You pick the sequence, the results only come from that context, and you decide the re-engagement angle per sequence. The "scan everything" approach mixes too many different

 lead types.

 Want me to rework it to take a sequence ID or name filter? Or do you want to approach this differently?

 ❯ I like # 2 AND 3, but i want you to enter plan mode and really think about the best ways we coudl do this.

 ⏺ Explore (Explore reengagement + Outreach sequences)

 ⏺ User approved Claude's plan

 ⎿  Plan saved to: ~/.claude/plans/generic-knitting-pebble.md · /plan to edit

 Plan: Redesign Sequence Re-Engagement as Report-Then-Act

 Context

 Current /prospect_reengagement auto-queues ALL finished/no-reply prospects from ALL sequences into one bucket. Wrong — a CUE booth ghost needs a different angle than a license request lurker. Steven wants

 to see what's available, pick a sequence, review prospects, then decide.

 Design

 One command, two modes. No new commands — reuses existing /prospect_approve .

 Mode 1: Overview (no args)

 /prospect_reengagement

 Lists all sequences with approximate no-reply counts. Uses ONLY outreach_client.get_sequences() — a single API call that returns num_contacted and num_replied per sequence. No expensive per-prospect

 scanning.

 🔄 *Sequence Re-Engagement Overview*

 1. CUE 2026 Booth — Teachers (ID 1985)

 2. !!!2026 License Request Seq (ID 1999)

 3. Algebra Webinar — Attendees (ID 2000)

 ...12 sequences total

 Pick one to scan: /prospect_reengagement 2

 num_contacted - num_replied is approximate (includes bounced, active, etc.) but good enough for picking which sequence to drill into. Caches list in _last_reengagement_sequences .

 Mode 2: Scan One Sequence (with index + optional segment)

 /prospect_reengagement 2

 /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker

 /prospect_reengagement 2 all

 Scans ONLY the selected sequence. Default segment: engaged (highest value — they clicked or opened 3+ times). Override with lurker , ghost , or all .

 🔄 Scanning: !!!2026 License Request Seq (ID 1999)

 Found 168 finished, no-reply prospects

 🟢 Engaged: 28 | 🟡 Lurkers: 67 | ⚪ Ghosts: 73

 1. Jane Smith (jane@springfield.k12.il.us) — Springfield SD, IL

 Opens: 5, Clicks: 2

 2. Bob Johnson (bjohnson@austinisd.org) — Austin ISD, TX

 Opens: 4, Clicks: 1

 ...showing top 10 of 28

 /prospect_approve 1,3,5 to queue selected

 /prospect_approve all to queue all 28

 /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker to see lurkers instead

 Sets _last_prospect_batch to the displayed prospects. Steven uses existing /prospect_approve and /prospect_skip — no new queue command needed.

 Argument parsing logic

 - No args → overview mode

 - Number <= 100 → index into _last_reengagement_sequences (from overview)

 - Number > 100 → treat as direct Outreach sequence ID

 - Optional second word: engaged (default), lurker , ghost , all

 Implementation

 tools/district_prospector.py — replace suggest_sequence_reengagement() with:

 1. get_sequence_reengagement_overview(exclude_sequence_ids=None) → dict

 def get_sequence_reengagement_overview(exclude_sequence_ids= None ) -> dict :

 - Calls outreach_client.get_sequences() — ONE API call

 - Filters: exclude C4 IDs {1995-1998}, exclude sequences with num_contacted == 0

 - For each sequence, compute: est_no_reply = num_contacted - num_replied

 - Returns: {success, sequences: [{id, name, num_contacted, num_replied, est_no_reply, reply_rate_pct}]}

 - Sorted by est_no_reply descending (biggest opportunity first)

 - No get_sequence_states() calls — fast

 2. scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment="engaged", progress_callback=None) → dict

 def scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment= "engaged" , progress_callback= None ) -> dict :

 - Calls outreach_client.get_sequence_states(sequence_id, include_prospect=True) — one sequence only

 - Filters to state == "finished" AND reply_count == 0

 - Segments each prospect: engaged (opens>=3 or clicks>0), lurker (opens>0), ghost (opens==0)

 - Applies exclusion filters: active customers, existing prospects, pipeline, territory check

 - Returns ALL candidates with segment labels, BUT only populates display list with requested segment

 - Returns: {success, sequence_name, total_no_reply, segment_counts: {engaged, lurker, ghost}, candidates: [prospect dicts for requested segment]}

 - Each candidate dict has: state, company, email, first_name, last_name, name_key, segment, priority, open_count, click_count, seq_name

 3. format_reengagement_overview(result) → str

 Formats the overview for Telegram. Numbered list with reply rate.

 4. format_reengagement_scan(result, segment) → str

 Formats the scan results. Shows segment counts at top, then numbered prospect list for the selected segment. Includes "/prospect_approve" hints.

 Delete the old suggest_sequence_reengagement() function entirely — it's being replaced, not refactored. The auto-queue behavior goes away.

 agent/main.py — modify handler

 New global:

 _last_reengagement_sequences: list [ dict ] = []

 Add to the global declaration line in handle_message() .

 Replace the current /prospect_reengagement handler with:

 elif user_text.lower().startswith( "/prospect_reengagement" ) or user_text.lower() in [ "reengagement" , "re-engagement" ]:

 parts = user_text.strip().split()

 # Mode 1: no args → overview

 # Mode 2: with number → scan specific sequence

 # Optional segment: engaged (default), lurker, ghost, all

 Parse logic:

 - Extract first numeric arg (if any) → seq_selector

 - Extract segment keyword (if any) → segment (default "engaged")

 - If no numeric arg: call get_sequence_reengagement_overview() , format, cache in _last_reengagement_sequences

 - If numeric arg <= 100: look up _last_reengagement_sequences[idx-1] , get sequence_id

 - If numeric arg > 100: use directly as sequence_id

 - Call scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment) , format, set _last_prospect_batch to candidates

 No changes to /prospect_approve or /prospect_skip — they already work with _last_prospect_batch .

 Files to Modify

 - tools/district_prospector.py — delete old function, add 4 new functions

 - agent/main.py — replace handler, add global

 - tools/CLAUDE.md — update function signatures

 - CLAUDE.md — update command docs

 Edge Cases

 - Overview returns 0 sequences with prospects → "No sequences with contacted prospects found."

 - Scan returns 0 no-reply for selected segment → "No {segment} prospects in this sequence. Try /prospect_reengagement {idx} all to see all segments."

 - _last_reengagement_sequences empty when Mode 2 attempted → "Run /prospect_reengagement first to see available sequences."

 - Outreach OAuth expired → outreach_client auto-refreshes; if refresh fails, returns auth error

 - Sequence has 5000+ prospects → get_sequence_states() handles pagination (50/page × 100 pages max). Add time.sleep(0.3) between page fetches won't work since pagination is internal to

 get_sequence_states() . Rate limiting is handled by the client.

 Verification

 1. /prospect_reengagement → shows sequence list with est. no-reply counts (fast, ~2 seconds)

 2. /prospect_reengagement 2 → scans one sequence, shows engaged prospects

 3. /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker → same sequence, lurker segment

 4. /prospect_reengagement 2 all → all segments

 5. /prospect_approve 1,3 → queues selected prospects from last scan

 6. Verify: NOTHING auto-queued at any step until explicit /prospect_approve

 Changes from v1

 ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Change │ Why │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Overview uses get_sequences() only — no per-prospect │ v1 called get_sequence_states() for every sequence in overview mode. With 30 sequences, that's 30+ paginated API calls just to show counts. │

 │ scanning │ Now it's 1 call. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Killed /reengagement_queue command │ Too close to /prospect_approve . Steven already knows approve/skip. Adding a third queue mechanism is confusing. Segment filtering moves to │

 │ │ scan time instead. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Segment selection at scan time, not queue time │ "Show me engaged from CUE Booth" is one step, not two. Reduces state management and cognitive overhead. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Removed _last_reengagement_scan global │ Fewer in-memory caches = fewer "run X first" errors. _last_prospect_batch handles everything. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Delete old function entirely, don't refactor │ Old function's auto-queue behavior is fundamentally wrong. Clean break is simpler than trying to preserve parts. │

 └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 ⏺ Pushed. After redeploy, test the new flow:

 1. /prospect_reengagement — should show your sequences with reply rates (fast, ~2 seconds)

 ⏺ You're right on both counts.

 1. Reply rate doesn't matter here. The point is finding sequences with prospects worth re-engaging. The useful number is "est. no-reply" — and it's already sorted by that. Reply rate is noise.

 2. 129 sequences is absurd to list. Most have 1-5 contacts. Only the top 10-15 with meaningful volume are worth scanning.

 Quick fix — filter to sequences with 20+ no-reply, show top 15 max, drop reply rate.

 ⏺ User approved Claude's plan

 ⎿  Plan saved to: ~/.claude/plans/generic-knitting-pebble.md · /plan to edit

 Plan: Redesign Sequence Re-Engagement as Report-Then-Act

 Context

 Current /prospect_reengagement auto-queues ALL finished/no-reply prospects from ALL sequences into one bucket. Wrong — a CUE booth ghost needs a different angle than a license request lurker. Steven wants

 to see what's available, pick a sequence, review prospects, then decide.

 Design

 One command, two modes. No new commands — reuses existing /prospect_approve .

 Mode 1: Overview (no args)

 /prospect_reengagement

 Lists all sequences with approximate no-reply counts. Uses ONLY outreach_client.get_sequences() — a single API call that returns num_contacted and num_replied per sequence. No expensive per-prospect

 scanning.

 🔄 *Sequence Re-Engagement Overview*

 1. CUE 2026 Booth — Teachers (ID 1985)

 2. !!!2026 License Request Seq (ID 1999)

 3. Algebra Webinar — Attendees (ID 2000)

 ...12 sequences total

 Pick one to scan: /prospect_reengagement 2

 num_contacted - num_replied is approximate (includes bounced, active, etc.) but good enough for picking which sequence to drill into. Caches list in _last_reengagement_sequences .

 Mode 2: Scan One Sequence (with index + optional segment)

 /prospect_reengagement 2

 /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker

 /prospect_reengagement 2 all

 Scans ONLY the selected sequence. Default segment: engaged (highest value — they clicked or opened 3+ times). Override with lurker , ghost , or all .

 🔄 Scanning: !!!2026 License Request Seq (ID 1999)

 Found 168 finished, no-reply prospects

 🟢 Engaged: 28 | 🟡 Lurkers: 67 | ⚪ Ghosts: 73

 1. Jane Smith (jane@springfield.k12.il.us) — Springfield SD, IL

 Opens: 5, Clicks: 2

 2. Bob Johnson (bjohnson@austinisd.org) — Austin ISD, TX

 Opens: 4, Clicks: 1

 ...showing top 10 of 28

 /prospect_approve 1,3,5 to queue selected

 /prospect_approve all to queue all 28

 /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker to see lurkers instead

 Sets _last_prospect_batch to the displayed prospects. Steven uses existing /prospect_approve and /prospect_skip — no new queue command needed.

 Argument parsing logic

 - No args → overview mode

 - Number <= 100 → index into _last_reengagement_sequences (from overview)

 - Number > 100 → treat as direct Outreach sequence ID

 - Optional second word: engaged (default), lurker , ghost , all

 Implementation

 tools/district_prospector.py — replace suggest_sequence_reengagement() with:

 1. get_sequence_reengagement_overview(exclude_sequence_ids=None) → dict

 def get_sequence_reengagement_overview(exclude_sequence_ids= None ) -> dict :

 - Calls outreach_client.get_sequences() — ONE API call

 - Filters: exclude C4 IDs {1995-1998}, exclude sequences with num_contacted == 0

 - For each sequence, compute: est_no_reply = num_contacted - num_replied

 - Returns: {success, sequences: [{id, name, num_contacted, num_replied, est_no_reply, reply_rate_pct}]}

 - Sorted by est_no_reply descending (biggest opportunity first)

 - No get_sequence_states() calls — fast

 2. scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment="engaged", progress_callback=None) → dict

 def scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment= "engaged" , progress_callback= None ) -> dict :

 - Calls outreach_client.get_sequence_states(sequence_id, include_prospect=True) — one sequence only

 - Filters to state == "finished" AND reply_count == 0

 - Segments each prospect: engaged (opens>=3 or clicks>0), lurker (opens>0), ghost (opens==0)

 - Applies exclusion filters: active customers, existing prospects, pipeline, territory check

 - Returns ALL candidates with segment labels, BUT only populates display list with requested segment

 - Returns: {success, sequence_name, total_no_reply, segment_counts: {engaged, lurker, ghost}, candidates: [prospect dicts for requested segment]}

 - Each candidate dict has: state, company, email, first_name, last_name, name_key, segment, priority, open_count, click_count, seq_name

 3. format_reengagement_overview(result) → str

 Formats the overview for Telegram. Numbered list with reply rate.

 4. format_reengagement_scan(result, segment) → str

 Formats the scan results. Shows segment counts at top, then numbered prospect list for the selected segment. Includes "/prospect_approve" hints.

 Delete the old suggest_sequence_reengagement() function entirely — it's being replaced, not refactored. The auto-queue behavior goes away.

 agent/main.py — modify handler

 New global:

 _last_reengagement_sequences: list [ dict ] = []

 Add to the global declaration line in handle_message() .

 Replace the current /prospect_reengagement handler with:

 elif user_text.lower().startswith( "/prospect_reengagement" ) or user_text.lower() in [ "reengagement" , "re-engagement" ]:

 parts = user_text.strip().split()

 # Mode 1: no args → overview

 # Mode 2: with number → scan specific sequence

 # Optional segment: engaged (default), lurker, ghost, all

 Parse logic:

 - Extract first numeric arg (if any) → seq_selector

 - Extract segment keyword (if any) → segment (default "engaged")

 - If no numeric arg: call get_sequence_reengagement_overview() , format, cache in _last_reengagement_sequences

 - If numeric arg <= 100: look up _last_reengagement_sequences[idx-1] , get sequence_id

 - If numeric arg > 100: use directly as sequence_id

 - Call scan_sequence_for_reengagement(sequence_id, segment) , format, set _last_prospect_batch to candidates

 No changes to /prospect_approve or /prospect_skip — they already work with _last_prospect_batch .

 Files to Modify

 - tools/district_prospector.py — delete old function, add 4 new functions

 - agent/main.py — replace handler, add global

 - tools/CLAUDE.md — update function signatures

 - CLAUDE.md — update command docs

 Edge Cases

 - Overview returns 0 sequences with prospects → "No sequences with contacted prospects found."

 - Scan returns 0 no-reply for selected segment → "No {segment} prospects in this sequence. Try /prospect_reengagement {idx} all to see all segments."

 - _last_reengagement_sequences empty when Mode 2 attempted → "Run /prospect_reengagement first to see available sequences."

 - Outreach OAuth expired → outreach_client auto-refreshes; if refresh fails, returns auth error

 - Sequence has 5000+ prospects → get_sequence_states() handles pagination (50/page × 100 pages max). Add time.sleep(0.3) between page fetches won't work since pagination is internal to

 get_sequence_states() . Rate limiting is handled by the client.

 Verification

 1. /prospect_reengagement → shows sequence list with est. no-reply counts (fast, ~2 seconds)

 2. /prospect_reengagement 2 → scans one sequence, shows engaged prospects

 3. /prospect_reengagement 2 lurker → same sequence, lurker segment

 4. /prospect_reengagement 2 all → all segments

 5. /prospect_approve 1,3 → queues selected prospects from last scan

 6. Verify: NOTHING auto-queued at any step until explicit /prospect_approve

 Changes from v1

 ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Change │ Why │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Overview uses get_sequences() only — no per-prospect │ v1 called get_sequence_states() for every sequence in overview mode. With 30 sequences, that's 30+ paginated API calls just to show counts. │

 │ scanning │ Now it's 1 call. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Killed /reengagement_queue command │ Too close to /prospect_approve . Steven already knows approve/skip. Adding a third queue mechanism is confusing. Segment filtering moves to │

 │ │ scan time instead. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Segment selection at scan time, not queue time │ "Show me engaged from CUE Booth" is one step, not two. Reduces state management and cognitive overhead. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Removed _last_reengagement_scan global │ Fewer in-memory caches = fewer "run X first" errors. _last_prospect_batch handles everything. │

 ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Delete old function entirely, don't refactor │ Old function's auto-queue behavior is fundamentally wrong. Clean break is simpler than trying to preserve parts. │

 └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 ⏺ You're right on both counts.

 1. Reply rate doesn't matter here. The point is finding sequences with prospects worth re-engaging. The useful number is "est. no-reply" — and it's already sorted by that. Reply rate is noise.

 2. 129 sequences is absurd to list. Most have 1-5 contacts. Only the top 10-15 with meaningful volume are worth scanning.

 Quick fix — filter to sequences with 20+ no-reply, show top 15 max, drop reply rate.

 ⏺ Pushed. After redeploy, try /prospect_reengagement 7 again — should now only show prospects from your 13 territory states, no international, no out-of-territory.

 ❯ while we wait please show me the full rules for creating email sequences

 ⏺ Here's everything — both files combined:

 ---

 VOICE & TONE

 - Zero trace of AI-written content. No formulaic structures, no ChatGPT-sounding phrasing

 - Stand out from the pack — if you've seen it in a sales blog, don't use it

 - No sales cliches: "just checking in", "circling back", "touch base", "reach out", "I'd love to", "quick call", "hop on a call"

 - No "I'm Steven" intros (signature handles it), no "sorry/dropped the ball" openers, no em dashes

 - Lead with student engagement, not product features. AI is supporting, not the lead

 - Conversational, direct — teachers are drowning in vendor emails

 - NEVER fabricate claims about schools/districts — Steven got burned by this

 - Frame as patterns: "most educators I work with find..." (Steven's preferred approach)

 - Real data: Bobby Duke MS case study, 99% teacher satisfaction, 20M+ students, 850K+ lines of code per student

 - Product accuracy: AI Junior = beta (NOT released), AI Algebra = launched, Cybersecurity = fall 2026

 VALUE PROPS (priority order)

 1. Engagement — video game look, rigorous curriculum, real Python/JS/C++ from day one

 2. Turn-key — zero-prep slides, no coding background needed (2/3 of teachers started with zero experience)

 3. Vertical alignment K-12 — replaces Scratch + Code.org + free tools patchwork

 4. Standards-aligned — CSTA, ISTE, Common Core, AP CSP endorsed

 5. Only coding esports in the world — safe, async, Chromebook-friendly

 6. CTE pathway (PCEP) — Python Institute cert, Perkins funding conversations

 7. AI curriculum (supporting) — AI HackStack, AI Algebra, Cybersecurity coming fall 2026

 8. Implementation support — PD, training, dedicated support

 9. 10 products in one suite

 - Step 1: under 80 words, single low-friction CTA (58% of replies come from Step 1)

 - Other steps: under 120 words

 - Breakup: under 60 words (pulls 20-30% reply rate)

 - Each step DISTINCT angle — never "just bumping"

 - Required angles: engagement, turn-key, esports/differentiator, info dump, budget/quote, breakup

 - ONE CTA per email, vary across steps

 - Variables: {{first_name}} , {{company}} , {{state}}

 - Minimum 5 days between all steps

 - Cold: 4-5 steps, graduated spacing (5-6-7 days), ~4 weeks total

 - Warm/upward: 3-4 steps, 5-6 days, 10-14 days total

 - Teachers: faster pace, more steps (4-8)

 - License requests: speed to lead — Step 1 fires immediately, Step 2 same day, then 5+ days

 SEND SCHEDULES

 - Teachers: Tue-Thu, 3 windows (6:30-8 AM, 12-1 PM, 3:30-4:30 PM)

 - Admins: Mon-Thu, 3 windows (6:30-8 AM, 10-11 AM, 3-5 PM)

 - Hot leads: Mon-Fri, 7 AM-4 PM

 LOW-FRICTION CTAs THAT WORK

 - "Would it be worth sending over some info?"

 - "Worth trying?"

 - "Just reply with your grade levels and I'll get it set up"

 - "Would it make sense to...?"

 SUBJECT LINES STEVEN LIKES

 - "students who won't stop coding"

 - "Chromebook esports?"

 - "the coding tool kids actually like"

 - "leftover budget idea for {{company}}"

 - "30-day trial, no strings"

 - <br><br> between paragraphs (NOT <p> tags)

 - ALL URLs must be hyperlinked

 - codecombat.com/schools hyperlinked in multiple steps

 - Plain text format preferred for deliverability

 - Intervals in SECONDS (5 days = 432000, 6 days = 518400)

 - to Recipients MUST be [] (empty) — NEVER ["{{to Recipient}}"]

 - Creation order: create sequence → Steven activates → toggle templates → add prospects

 - Owner: Steven (ID 11), sharing: private, throttle: 150/day

 SEASONAL (current: Budget Season Feb-Jun)

 - Districts finalizing budgets for next fiscal year

 - Grant writing: Title I, CTE/Perkins, Title IV-A

 - Leftover funds need to be spent before they expire

 - "Get a quote in hand so you can start having those conversations"
